## Update (2026-04-13) - Enrollment Stripe Link Field + Non-Clipping Country Dropdown + No Panel Scroll

### What Was Changed

- Added a visible Stripe Link Authentication element to enrollment Step 3 so Link/autofill is explicitly available in the panel UI.
- Updated enrollment custom-select runtime to use floating menu behavior:
  - menu is moved to `document.body` while open
  - fixed-position placement follows trigger coordinates
  - avoids clipping inside enrollment panel/container boundaries
- Updated custom-select open/close state handling and outside-click logic to support floating menus safely.
- Removed enrollment panel body scroll behavior by switching modal body overflow to hidden (no side scroll functionality in panel flow).

### Files Affected

- `binary-tree-next-app.mjs`
- `binary-tree-next.html`
- `Claude_Notes/charge-documentation.md`
- `Claude_Notes/Current Project Status.md`

### Design Decisions

- Used floating-menu placement instead of in-panel absolute menus to solve clipping without compromising themed dropdown style.
- Kept country-list internal menu scroll only for the floating dropdown itself while disabling panel/page scroll.

### Known Limitations

- With panel scroll disabled, extremely short viewport heights can visually compress Step 3 layout.
- Stripe Link visibility still depends on Stripe/session eligibility even with Link Authentication element mounted.

### Validation

- `node --check binary-tree-next-app.mjs` passed.

## Update (2026-04-13) - Enrollment Billing Country Themed Select + Stripe Link Autofill Parity

### What Was Changed

- Replaced enrollment Step-3 `Billing Country` text input with the same custom dropdown pattern used by Enrollment panel selects.
- Added runtime country hydration for billing-country options from local catalog:
  - source: `/node_modules/flag-icons/country.json`
  - uses ISO alpha-2 country codes as select values
  - labels shown in-country name form
  - fallback remains `United States` when catalog load fails
- Added dropdown scrolling support for long country lists (`max-height` + `overflow-y`) so the panel keeps the same layout while supporting full country coverage.
- Updated submit billing-country handling to use selected ISO code directly for Stripe billing details.
- Enabled Stripe Link/autofill parity behavior on enrollment card number element config by explicitly keeping Link enabled (`disableLink: false`) with icon display.

### Files Affected

- `binary-tree-next-app.mjs`
- `binary-tree-next.html`
- `Claude_Notes/charge-documentation.md`
- `Claude_Notes/Current Project Status.md`

### Design Decisions

- Reused existing enrollment custom-select interaction model to keep billing-country UI visually and behaviorally consistent with Step 2 fields.
- Used local country catalog hydration instead of hardcoding hundreds of options in HTML to keep markup clean and maintainable.
- Kept ISO country-code values in the select model to align with Stripe billing address expectations.

### Known Limitations

- Country list is sourced from ISO country catalog; Stripe market availability can differ by product/region capability.
- Stripe Link/autofill UI appears only when Stripe determines the customer/session is eligible.

### Validation

- `node --check binary-tree-next-app.mjs` passed.

## Update (2026-04-13) - Profile Popup Home Redirect + Enrollment Step-3 Stripe/Billing Upgrade

### What Was Changed

- Updated Binary Tree Next profile popup `Home` action behavior so it redirects to dashboard home page instead of resetting the tree camera.
  - member source now routes to `/index.html`
  - admin source now routes to `/admin.html`
- Expanded Enrollment panel Step 3 card section to match Store checkout field scope:
  - retained Stripe card number field with icon
  - added Stripe expiry + CVC support
  - added Billing Address inputs (address, city, state, ZIP/postal, country)
- Upgraded Stripe validation flow in enrollment submit:
  - requires complete card number, expiry, and CVC
  - requires complete billing address set before submit
  - creates a Stripe Payment Method (`createPaymentMethod`) using card + billing details before registration API call
  - includes `stripePaymentMethodId` and billing snapshot in enrollment payload for backend continuity

### Files Affected

- `binary-tree-next-app.mjs`
- `binary-tree-next.html`
- `Claude_Notes/charge-documentation.md`
- `Claude_Notes/Current Project Status.md`

### Design Decisions

- Kept `camera:home` behavior unchanged so the in-canvas home camera reset remains available as a separate control.
- Used split Stripe Elements (`cardNumber`, `cardExpiry`, `cardCvc`) to preserve the existing custom panel layout while adding CVC support.
- Added a lightweight country-to-ISO resolver focused on U.S. aliases so billing can pass Stripe-compatible country codes where available.

### Known Limitations

- Enrollment backend currently stores registration data; it does not yet perform a server-side Stripe payment-intent confirmation for this enrollment flow.
- Country-code aliasing in this pass is intentionally minimal (primarily U.S. variants) and can be expanded later if needed.

### Validation

- `node --check binary-tree-next-app.mjs` passed.

## Update (2026-04-10) - First-Time Binary Tree Next Launch Check + Welcome Tap Gate

### What Was Changed

- Added backend persistence and launch-state tracking for Binary Tree Next first-open behavior:
  - new store `backend/stores/member-binary-tree-intro.store.js`
  - tracks first-open and last-open timestamps per member user id.
- Added authenticated member API endpoint to resolve launch state:
  - `GET /api/member-auth/binary-tree-next/launch-state`
  - returns `firstTime`, `firstOpenedAt`, `lastOpenedAt`, `checkedAt`.
- Integrated launch-state fetch into `binary-tree-next-app.mjs` member bootstrap flow.
- Added first-time welcome splash gate to next-gen page:
  - shows `Welcome` and `Press the screen to continue.`
  - displayed only for first-time member opens
  - waits for screen tap/click (or Enter/Space) before starting intro animation.
- Returning members (and admin source) keep normal startup flow with no extra prompt.

### Files Affected

- `backend/stores/member-binary-tree-intro.store.js`
- `backend/services/auth.service.js`
- `backend/controllers/auth.controller.js`
- `backend/routes/auth.routes.js`
- `binary-tree-next-app.mjs`
- `binary-tree-next.html`
- `Claude_Notes/charge-documentation.md`
- `Claude_Notes/Current Project Status.md`
- `Claude_Notes/binary-tree-next-gen-wasm-plan.md`

### Design Decisions

- Kept launch-state endpoint under existing member-auth session middleware to avoid introducing a separate auth path.
- Implemented fail-open behavior in frontend launch-state fetch:
  - if endpoint/network fails, app falls back to normal startup (`firstTime: false`) so tree boot is never blocked.
- Welcome gate is shown after loading-screen completion and before intro animation start, matching requested sequence.

### Known Limitations

- The first-open prompt currently applies to member source only; admin source explicitly skips this flow.
- Splash text/action is currently fixed copy and not yet localized.

### Validation

- `node --check backend/stores/member-binary-tree-intro.store.js` passed.
- `node --check backend/services/auth.service.js` passed.
- `node --check backend/controllers/auth.controller.js` passed.
- `node --check backend/routes/auth.routes.js` passed.
- `node --check binary-tree-next-app.mjs` passed.

## Update (2026-04-10) - Left Shell Logo Edge Quality Fix (Canvas Rendering)

### What Was Changed

- Improved left-shell brand logo rendering quality in canvas (`drawSideNavBrandLogo()`):
  - forced `imageSmoothingEnabled = true`
  - forced `imageSmoothingQuality = 'high'`
  - aligned draw coordinates/sizes to device-pixel increments based on DPR before `drawImage()`.

### Files Affected

- `binary-tree-next-app.mjs`
- `Claude_Notes/charge-documentation.md`
- `Claude_Notes/Current Project Status.md`
- `Claude_Notes/binary-tree-next-gen-wasm-plan.md`

### Reasoning

- Dashboard logo appears cleaner because it is browser DOM image rendering, while this shell uses per-frame canvas raster scaling.
- High-quality smoothing + DPR pixel alignment reduces edge jaggies in canvas downscaling.

### Validation

- `node --check binary-tree-next-app.mjs` passed.

## Update (2026-04-10) - Binary Tree Next Select Animation Bugfix (Both Directions)

### What Was Changed

- Fixed node selection animation so select and deselect both animate.
- Root cause:
  - select animation track was starting from `1 -> 1` because selected state was set before capture.
- Fix applied:
  - `startSelectionAnimation()` now supports `fromOverride`
  - select path explicitly starts from `0 -> 1`
  - deselect path remains `current -> 0`.

- `binary-tree-next-app.mjs`

## Update (2026-04-10) - Binary Tree Next Fullscreen Glass Shell + Contact Nodes

### What Was Changed

- Reworked the next-gen shell to remove fixed left/right outer gutters and restore a fullscreen canvas viewport.
- Added an in-canvas side navigation panel that can be shown or hidden via a floating toggle button.
- Applied a dark-gray glassmorphism treatment across shell surfaces (background, side nav, top bar, bottom bar, buttons).
- Updated node visuals to circular contact-style avatars with centered initials only (full + medium LOD).
- Expanded culling tolerance using dynamic cullMargin in the engine adapter to reduce premature line/node pop-out while panning.
- Blocked canvas pan/zoom/select interactions while pointer is inside the open side nav overlay.

- Claude_Notes/charge-documentation.md

- Claude_Notes/binary-tree-next-gen-wasm-plan.md

### Design Decisions

- Fullscreen viewport is now the projection source of truth, keeping render/culling aligned with visible canvas area.
- Shell remains canvas-native so future wasm integration does not need a DOM-shell rewrite.
- Initials-only node avatars are implemented now to lock down target visual language before live tree-logic migration.

### Known Limitations

- Legacy drawRightPanel() remains in source as inactive code and is not rendered.
- No screenshot comparison pass was executed in this change; validation is based on syntax checks and code-path review.

### Validation

- node --check binary-tree-next-app.mjs passed.
- node --check binary-tree-next-engine-adapter.mjs passed.

## Update (2026-04-10) - Binary Tree Next Figma-Style Canvas Chrome Redesign

### What Was Changed

- Replaced the prior mixed DOM-panel shell with a full-canvas UI composition for `binary-tree-next`:
  - left and right workspace panels are now rendered directly in canvas
  - center workspace remains the interactive tree canvas region
  - added a floating center status bar and a bottom-center tool panel (Figma-style).
- Updated `binary-tree-next.html` to a minimal full-viewport canvas host:
  - `#figma-tree-canvas`
  - optional `#boot-error` overlay for bootstrap failures only
  - no persistent DOM side panels/toolbars.
- Rebuilt `binary-tree-next-app.mjs` rendering model around canvas-drawn chrome:
  - Figma-like left panel (file/assets/pages/layers sections)
  - Figma-like right properties panel (design/prototype tabs + section blocks)
  - center floating status/actions bar (`Legacy`, `Fit`)
  - bottom-center tool panel with active tool highlighting
  - workspace clipping so tree drawing stays in center viewport only.
- Preserved/updated runtime interactions:
  - pan/zoom/select node interactions in center workspace
  - fit-to-view support (`Fit` action + keyboard `F`)
  - keyboard pan/zoom/reset shortcuts maintained
  - auth/session boot checks retained for member/admin source modes.
- Updated engine adapter projection options to support custom viewport anchors:
  - `viewport.centerX`
  - `viewport.baseY`
  - `nodeRadiusBase`
  This allows tree layout to be centered in the middle workspace instead of full-window center.

### Design Decisions

- Prioritized visual parity with the provided Figma reference structure over prior dashboard-card styling.
- Moved panel chrome into the render surface itself to match the requested ????????????????front panels rendered in canvas?????? behavior.
- Kept interactive behavior modular (`binary-tree-next-engine-adapter.mjs`) so this visual pass does not block future wasm engine wiring.

### Known Limitations

- This pass focuses on Figma-style shell composition and interaction model; it is not yet pixel-perfect replica detail-for-detail of every micro-icon/label in the reference.
- Tree data is still mock-data backed pending next data-contract integration pass.

### Files Affected

- `binary-tree-next.html`
- `binary-tree-next-app.mjs`
- `binary-tree-next-engine-adapter.mjs`
- `Claude_Notes/binary-tree-next-gen-wasm-plan.md`

- `Claude_Notes/charge-documentation.md`

- `node --check binary-tree-next-engine-adapter.mjs` passed.

## Update (2026-04-10) - Binary Tree Next Phase B Shell Controls + Adapter Module Split

### What Was Changed

- Continued Binary Tree Next implementation beyond Phase A shell:
  - split inline runtime logic out of `binary-tree-next.html` into ES modules:
    - `binary-tree-next-app.mjs`
    - `binary-tree-next-engine-adapter.mjs`
  - migrated page bootstrap to module entry:
    - `binary-tree-next.html` now uses `<script type="module" src="/binary-tree-next-app.mjs">`
- Added renderer-shell controls in new window app:
  - camera buttons: `Zoom +`, `Zoom -`, `Fit`
  - viewport toggles:
    - show connectors
    - show guide grid
    - highlight high-volume nodes
  - keyboard interaction support:
    - `+` / `-` zoom
    - `0` reset
    - `F` fit
    - arrow-key panning
- Added adapter boundary for upcoming Wasm integration:
  - `createBinaryTreeNextEngineAdapter()` now owns:
    - node filtering
    - layout projection
    - connector frame generation
    - visible-world bounds for fit-to-view
  - `detectBinaryTreeNextEngineMode()` probes runtime support and wasm artifact presence:
    - reports `mock-js` or `wasm-bridge-pending`
  - diagnostics now include explicit engine mode in UI.

### Design Decisions

- Kept same-origin, no-build ES module approach to avoid introducing bundler/toolchain risk before Wasm engine milestones.
- Introduced an adapter contract now so C++/Wasm layout core can later replace mock JS compute path with minimal UI-shell churn.

### Known Limitations

- Adapter currently computes in JS mock mode; no live C++/Wasm runtime execution yet.
- Wasm detection is artifact/runtime capability probing only; it does not initialize a compiled module yet.

- `binary-tree-next.html`

## Update (2026-04-10) - Binary Tree Next Phase A Scaffold Implemented

### What Was Changed

- Implemented Phase A foundation work for the new isolated Binary Tree app:
  - added new standalone page: `binary-tree-next.html`
  - added explicit backend route mapping:
    - `/binary-tree-next`
    - `/binary-tree-next/`
    - `/binary-tree-next.html`
- Added launcher buttons from existing Binary Tree surfaces:
  - member app (`index.html`) now includes `Next-Gen` action in tree header controls
  - admin app (`admin.html`) now includes matching `Next-Gen` action
  - launcher behavior opens new window with source context:
    - member: `/binary-tree-next?source=member`
    - admin: `/binary-tree-next?source=admin`
- Added boot-time protected-route/session checks in `binary-tree-next.html`:
  - member path validates bearer token against `GET /api/member-auth/email-verification-status`
  - invalid/missing member session redirects to `/login.html`
  - admin source path checks admin session snapshot and redirects to `/admin-login.html` if missing
- Added mock isolated render harness in new page:
  - header rail with session identity + quick actions
  - left tool dock (`search`, depth filter)
  - center viewport with interactive canvas mock tree (select/pan/zoom)
  - right detail panel with selected-node metric snapshot
  - bottom diagnostics strip (mode, auth status, fps, visible nodes, selected node).

### Design Decisions

- Kept legacy Binary Tree unchanged and available while next-gen app runs in parallel.
- Kept launch flow same-origin and route-based to preserve browser session continuity.
- Used lightweight mock canvas harness in Phase A to validate isolated window shell + session guard before Wasm integration.

### Known Limitations

- Admin source validation is local-session based only (no dedicated admin session-validation API endpoint yet).
- Current `/binary-tree-next` viewport uses mock node data; backend data wiring is deferred to later phases.
- Wasm engine integration is not part of this phase.

## Update (2026-04-09) - Next-Gen Binary Tree (WASM) Plan Documented

  - keep current Binary Tree active for live usage/testing
  - build a new Binary Tree in a separate external window/page.
- Documented proposed architecture for the new track:
  - C++ computational core compiled to WebAssembly
  - TypeScript/optional React shell for surrounding controls/UI

  - renderer/interaction skeleton
  - Wasm layout core

- Kept old-tree fallback as a first-class rollout requirement.
- Scoped initial Wasm responsibilities to layout/visibility/path generation first, leaving UI shell logic in JS/TS.

### Known Limitations

- This update is planning-only and does not modify the current runtime engine.
- Specific compiler toolchain choices (Emscripten vs alternatives) remain open until execution phase.

## Update (2026-04-08) - Local Branch Verification + Popup Cover Visibility Fix

### What Was Changed

- Reproduced the node-popup cover issue directly on local branch with scripted Binary Tree interaction runs (Puppeteer).
- Root cause confirmed in popup positioning behavior:
  - when the selected node is near the top region, popup placement logic forced an above-node layout with a negative top value
  - the popup cover strip (top section) was clipped out of the visible canvas region, making cover images appear missing even when loaded.
- Fixed selected-node popup placement in `binary-tree.mjs`:
  - popup now falls back to below-node placement when there is not enough space above
  - pointer rendering now supports both orientations (downward when popup is above node, upward when popup is below node)
  - viewport clamping now preserves full popup card visibility (including cover strip).
- Kept cover-strip rendering path aligned with prior cleanup goals:
  - top-strip mask remains active to prevent radius bleed
  - overlay switches to image mode only after texture readiness checks.

### Design Decisions

- Prioritized full-card visibility over always-above-node anchoring.
- Added bidirectional pointer behavior instead of forcing clipped above placement, so cover/media and header identity rows remain visible in all node positions.

### Known Limitations

- Relative URL cover sources can still depend on runtime asset accessibility and load timing; data-url profile uploads are the most robust path in current implementation.

### Files Affected

- `binary-tree.mjs`
- `Claude_Notes/charge-documentation.md`
- `Claude_Notes/Current Project Status.md`
- `Claude_Notes/binary-tree-business-center.md`

### Validation

- `node --check binary-tree.mjs` passed.
- Local branch visual verification screenshots:
  - issue reproduction (cover visually missing/clipped): `temporary screenshots/screenshot-188-cover-check-local-image.png`
  - post-fix confirmation with forced bright cover payload: `temporary screenshots/screenshot-192-cover-check-yellow-canvas.png`.

## Update (2026-04-08) - Node Popup Cover Reliability Pass (Prevent False "Loaded" State)

### What Was Changed

- Fixed selected-node popup cover loading in `binary-tree.mjs` so the UI only switches to the low-overlay "image loaded" style after the texture is truly ready.
- Added a guarded texture-readiness flow:
  - normalize PIXI texture candidates
  - wait for base texture validity (`loaded`/`update`) or fail on `error`/timeout
  - keep procedural fallback overlay if the image cannot be confirmed.
- Expanded popup cover source fallback in the popup renderer to accept:
  - `profileCoverUrl`
  - `coverDataUrl`
  - `coverUrl`
  This prevents legacy/mixed node payload shapes from silently losing their cover source.

### Design Decisions

- Prioritized resilient runtime behavior over optimistic loading: we now treat "has URL" and "image successfully rendered" as separate states.
- Kept fallback artwork visible until texture readiness is confirmed to avoid the "cover disappeared" visual regression.

### Known Limitations

- This pass does not add network retry/backoff for failed remote image URLs; it keeps fallback cover styling instead.
- End-to-end visual verification requires authenticated in-app popup testing because static screenshot routes may not preserve live member session state.

### Files Affected

- `binary-tree.mjs`
- `Claude_Notes/charge-documentation.md`
- `Claude_Notes/Current Project Status.md`
- `Claude_Notes/binary-tree-business-center.md`

### Validation

- `node --check binary-tree.mjs` passed.

## Update (2026-04-08) - Cover Sync Investigation + Popup Render Fix

### What Was Changed

- Investigated why node popup cover changes were not visible and found two causes:
  - popup cover image was being painted beneath a near-opaque procedural tint layer
  - many non-root nodes have no backend-provided cover field yet.
- Fixed stale runtime-sync gap:
  - profile image uploads/saves updated profile UI state but did not rebuild tree node payloads immediately
  - added immediate `syncBinaryTreeFromRegisteredMembers()` calls after profile image update and profile save paths so popup cover updates in-session without requiring a reload.
- `binary-tree.mjs` popup render fix:
  - reduced procedural cover tint opacity when a node cover image exists
  - increased cover sprite alpha so custom covers are visibly rendered.
- Fallback data hydration added for node covers:
  - `index.html` now checks `charge-member-profile-customizations-v1` for a member-matching profile cover when API member data has no cover
  - `admin.html` now does the same for admin-side tree rendering.

### Files Affected

- `binary-tree.mjs`
- `index.html`
- `admin.html`
- `Claude_Notes/charge-documentation.md`
- `Claude_Notes/Current Project Status.md`
- `Claude_Notes/binary-tree-business-center.md`

### Design Decisions

- Kept existing popup visual style and used layered tint reduction instead of removing brand tint entirely, so custom images show while preserving card theme consistency.
- Implemented local-profile-store fallback for immediate node-cover improvement without requiring a backend schema migration first.

### Known Limitations

- Cross-device/global cover sync for all members is still limited by backend profile persistence: member profile cover fields are not yet stored/served in `registered_members` API payloads.
- Current fallback works for profiles saved in the local browser customization store.

### Validation

- `node --check binary-tree.mjs` passed.
- Screenshot probe on `http://127.0.0.1:5500` failed (`ERR_EMPTY_RESPONSE`) in this environment; server validation was run against `http://127.0.0.1:3000`.

## Update (2026-04-08) - Dynamic Popup Height For Metrics Capacity

### What Was Changed

- Implemented dynamic selected-node popup height in `binary-tree.mjs` so the panel can grow when metrics need more space.
- Added runtime height calculation based on:
  - divider and metrics panel start
  - minimum required metrics panel height
  - bottom padding budget.
- Updated popup rendering/positioning to use computed height end-to-end:
  - popup shadow + surface redraw when expanded
  - metrics panel height calculation from dynamic popup height
  - cycle-status footer anchored from dynamic bottom
  - pointer and viewport anchoring use `state.selectedNodePopup.popupHeight`.
- Result: avoids lower-row clipping/crowding (`Cycles` / `Direct`) while preserving requested section order and spacing hierarchy.

### Files Affected

- `binary-tree.mjs`
- `Claude_Notes/charge-documentation.md`
- `Claude_Notes/Current Project Status.md`
- `Claude_Notes/binary-tree-business-center.md`

### Validation

- `node --check binary-tree.mjs` passed.
- Screenshot verification not run in this sub-pass (focused geometry correction + compile validation).

- Refined binary-tree node popup styling in `binary-tree.mjs` using a Discord-inspired profile-card direction (without copying exact UI):

## Update (2026-04-08) - Node Profile Popup Anchored Above Selected Node

### What Was Changed

- Added node-click profile popup rendering in `binary-tree.mjs` with:
  - cover header treatment
  - profile avatar initials
  - rank/status chips
  - binary metrics (`Left Team`, `Right Team`, `Cycles`, `Direct`) plus cycle-eligibility state.
- Popup now anchors to the selected node and stays positioned above it while:
  - panning
  - zooming
  - animated focus camera moves
  - minimap-driven camera moves
  - viewport resize.
- Implemented popup lifecycle handling:
  - create/rebuild on node selection and visual refresh
  - cleanup on tree clear/empty state/destroy
  - rebuild-safe theme sync support.
- Updated camera and renderer hooks so popup position is continuously refreshed in sync with world transform.

### Files Affected

- `binary-tree.mjs`
- `Claude_Notes/charge-documentation.md`
- `Claude_Notes/Current Project Status.md`
- `Claude_Notes/binary-tree-business-center.md`

### Design Decisions

- Popup is rendered in a dedicated stage overlay layer (`selectedNodePopupLayer`) so it behaves like a UI overlay while still anchoring to world-space node positions.
- Popup only appears when the selected node is currently visible in the active render set, preventing stale misplaced overlays during cascade/LOD filtering.

### Known Limitations

- If a selected node is very close to the viewport top edge, the popup is still constrained for readability and may render with reduced pointer length.
- Popup is view-only in this pass (no action buttons yet).

### Validation

- `node --check binary-tree.mjs` passed.

---

## Update (2026-04-08) - Consistent Deep Anticipation Placement Length

### What Was Changed

- Refined selected-node anticipation positioning in `binary-tree.mjs` to prevent misleading long/short deep-slot connectors.
- Added selected-parent local placement mode inside `resolveEnrollAnticipationPositions(...)`:
  - when anticipation slots belong to one selected parent, placeholders are anchored with fixed left/right horizontal offset from that parent
  - both sides remain visually symmetric and near the selected branch context.
- Added collision handling for local placement:
  - checks collisions against existing rendered node positions
  - resolves conflicts primarily by stepping anticipation nodes downward (not by extreme horizontal drift)
  - only applies a small side nudge as last fallback.
- Result:
  - reduces cases where depth-6/7 anticipation lines become overly long/short and look like wrong placement zones.

### Files Affected

- `binary-tree.mjs`
- `Claude_Notes/charge-documentation.md`
- `Claude_Notes/Current Project Status.md`
- `Claude_Notes/binary-tree-business-center.md`

### Validation

- `node --check binary-tree.mjs` passed.

## Update (2026-04-08) - Selected-Leaf Anticipation Trigger

### What Was Changed

- Changed anticipated-node trigger behavior in `binary-tree.mjs`:
  - anticipated slots now appear only for the currently selected node
  - selected node must be a leaf (no left child and no right child).
- Updated selection flow so fullscreen node taps immediately re-render tree state:
  - ensures anticipation slots show/hide right after selecting/clearing nodes
  - avoids stale anticipation overlays from previous selections.
- Added explicit guard logic in `collectEnrollAnticipationSlots(...)`:
  - if selected node has any child, no anticipated slots are rendered
  - if selected node is a leaf, both left/right anticipated slots are generated under that node.

- `binary-tree.mjs`
- `Claude_Notes/charge-documentation.md`
- `Claude_Notes/Current Project Status.md`

## Update (2026-04-08) - Cascading Chunk Reveal By Zoom

### What Was Changed

- Implemented map-style cascade visibility logic in `binary-tree.mjs` for fullscreen tree browsing:
  - baseline global visibility keeps shallow structure visible (`depth <= 4`)
  - deeper levels are revealed only inside focused viewport chunks as zoom increases.
- Cascade depth behavior now follows LOD zoom thresholds:
  - far: depth up to 4
  - mid: depth up to 6 (focused chunk expansion)
  - near: full depth (focused chunk expansion).
- Added viewport-chunk resolver:
  - finds depth-4 chunk roots inside expanded viewport bounds
  - reveals descendants under those chunk roots up to active zoom depth limit.
- Added selection continuity safeguards:
  - selected node + ancestor chain are always retained in visible set
  - selecting a node outside current visible chunk triggers re-render before focus so navigation remains reliable.
- Updated render pipeline to use visible-node subset for:
  - normal node drawing
  - parent-child links
  - spillover cache/link rendering
  - anticipation slot generation (visible parents only).

### Files Affected

- `binary-tree.mjs`
- `Claude_Notes/charge-documentation.md`
- `Claude_Notes/Current Project Status.md`
- `Claude_Notes/binary-tree-business-center.md`

### Validation

- `node --check binary-tree.mjs` passed.

## Update (2026-04-08) - Enroll Mode Layout Shift Fix

### What Was Changed

- Investigated Enroll Member layout regression in `binary-tree.mjs`.
- Root cause:
  - Enroll mode render path switched `computeLayout(...)` into legacy full-slot spacing (`reserveMissingChildDepth: true`, larger slot width, uncapped width depth), causing a visible jump to older tree geometry.
- Applied fix:
  - Enroll mode now uses the same compact layout parameters as normal browsing.
  - Anticipated nodes still render in enroll mode, but as an overlay on the current geometry rather than triggering legacy spacing.

- `binary-tree.mjs`
- `Claude_Notes/charge-documentation.md`

- `Claude_Notes/binary-tree-business-center.md`

## Update (2026-04-08) - Maps-Style Semantic Zoom For Binary Tree (LOD Pass 1)

### What Was Changed

- Reworked binary tree interaction toward a map-style navigation model in `binary-tree.mjs`:
  - restored stable slot-based geometry (removed structural distortion experiments)
  - added semantic zoom levels (LOD) for normal browsing:
    - `far` -> show depth `<= 3`
    - `mid` -> show depth `<= 5`
    - `near` -> show full depth.
- Added zoom hysteresis to prevent flicker while wheel/pinch zooming:
  - far enter/exit: `0.48 / 0.58`
  - near enter/exit: `0.72 / 0.64`.
- Added hidden descendant summary chips on visible frontier nodes during far/mid zoom:
  - shows `+N more` to indicate collapsed deeper nodes.
- Updated camera interactions so LOD refreshes automatically on scale changes:
  - wheel zoom
  - animated focus navigation
  - fit/reset view
  - persisted camera restore.
- Updated rendering/bounds behavior to align with LOD:
  - draw links only for currently visible nodes
  - spillover cache/lines now filter to visible nodes
  - fit bounds derive from visible nodes in current LOD mode for more usable initial framing.

### Files Affected

- `binary-tree.mjs`
- `Claude_Notes/charge-documentation.md`
- `Claude_Notes/Current Project Status.md`
- `Claude_Notes/binary-tree-business-center.md`

### Design Decisions

- Kept map-style principle: stable world coordinates + camera/zoom-driven visibility, not geometry warping.
- LOD is disabled during enroll anticipation mode so placeholder-slot projection remains exact.
- Preserved selected-node context by forcing selected path visibility when needed.

### Known Limitations

- This pass uses depth-based LOD, not full subtree cluster node aggregation yet.
- Near-threshold tuning may still require UX calibration after live review.

### Validation

- `node --check binary-tree.mjs` passed.

---

## Update (2026-04-08) - LOD-Driven Width Compression (Pass 2)

### What Was Changed

- Tightened binary tree horizontal spread in `binary-tree.mjs` by making layout width depth-aware to current LOD mode.
- Added `widthDepthCap` support in `computeLayout(...)`:
  - width formula now uses capped effective depth for layout width only
  - keeps original slot-based geometry/direction intact.
- Updated `renderTree()` to compute LOD mode first, then pass depth cap to layout:
  - `far`/`mid` now receive narrower world width aligned to visible semantic zoom depth
  - reduces excessive horizontal spacing while preserving node order/shape.

### Files Affected

- `binary-tree.mjs`
- `Claude_Notes/charge-documentation.md`
- `Claude_Notes/Current Project Status.md`
- `Claude_Notes/binary-tree-business-center.md`

### Validation

- `node --check binary-tree.mjs` passed.

## Update (2026-04-08) - Binary Tree Horizontal Compaction (Usability Fix)

### What Was Changed

- Updated binary tree layout behavior in `binary-tree.mjs` to reduce excessive horizontal spread for sparse trees.
- Refined compaction approach after UX feedback:
  - reverted prior structural compaction experiments after visual regressions
  - final approach keeps original layout geometry and applies horizontal x-axis compression only for sparse trees.
- Introduced dual layout modes inside `computeLayout(...)`:
  - **Geometry-preserving compression mode (default tree browsing)**:
    - original slot-based tree geometry retained
    - adaptive horizontal scale (based on tree occupancy) compresses width without changing branch direction.
  - **Full-slot mode (enroll anticipation mode only)**:
    - keeps previous power-of-two slot spacing
    - preserves accurate placeholder slot projection for enroll workflow.

### Files Affected

- `binary-tree.mjs`
- `Claude_Notes/charge-documentation.md`
- `Claude_Notes/Current Project Status.md`
- `Claude_Notes/binary-tree-business-center.md`

### Design Decisions

- Scoped compaction to normal render mode only to avoid breaking enroll anticipation placeholder geometry.
- Kept existing depth/slot metadata intact for compatibility with downstream placeholder calculations.

### Known Limitations

- Very deep trees can still become tall vertically; this pass targets horizontal density/usability only.

### Validation

- `node --check binary-tree.mjs` passed.

---

## Update (2026-04-08) - Randomized 50-Node Binary Tree Live Test (Zeroone)

### What Was Changed

- Recreated and replaced missing simulation runner:
  - added `backend/scripts/simulate-zeroone-live-test.mjs` with integrated reset + live-test execution flow.
- Updated test generation logic to match requested behavior:
  - creates exactly `50 total nodes` (not 50 direct sponsors)
  - randomized sponsor assignment across newly created members
  - randomized direct sponsor count target and left/right spread
  - excludes `preferred-customer-pack` from generated nodes.
- Added realistic account activity simulation:
  - randomized paid package enrollment mix
  - randomized purchase PV gains for all generated nodes
  - randomized paid-tier upgrades for a subset of generated nodes.
- Added end-to-end KPI reporting for `zeroone`:
  - binary tree volumes + cycles
  - sales team commission snapshot
  - force server cutoff results
  - achievement/reward progression
  - Infinity Builder / Legacy Leadership tier-card summaries.

### Files Affected

- `backend/scripts/simulate-zeroone-live-test.mjs`
- `Claude_Notes/charge-documentation.md`
- `Claude_Notes/Current Project Status.md`
- `Claude_Notes/binary-tree-business-center.md`

### Test Result Snapshot

- Run ID: `zeroone:20260408012540525-tstltn`
- Report file:
  - `backend/scripts/reports/zeroone-live-test-zeroone-20260408012540525-tstltn.json`
- Generated nodes:
  - total created: `50`
  - direct under `zeroone`: `3`
  - requested direct split: left `1`, right `2`
  - package distribution: personal `11`, business `11`, infinity `8`, legacy `20`.
- Core metrics for `zeroone`:
  - left BV `20,426`
  - right BV `45,334`
  - cycles `40`
  - total accumulated PV `65,760`
  - sales team net commission `2,500 USD`
  - fast-track commission from direct enrollments `342.40`.

### Design Decisions

- Added built-in reset behavior before simulation to prevent stale snapshots from polluting test reads.
- Kept account creation via service-layer enrollment for realistic sponsor/placement handling.
- Applied purchase/upgrade updates in bulk write pass to keep runtime practical at 50-node scale.

### Known Limitations

- Run duration is still heavy due existing enrollment write pattern in current service/store architecture.
- Direct sponsor totals shown in achievement summaries can include pre-existing account state, not only newly generated nodes.

### Validation

- `node --check backend/scripts/simulate-zeroone-live-test.mjs` passed.
- `node backend/scripts/simulate-zeroone-live-test.mjs --target=zeroone --reset-only` passed.
- Full run completed with report output at:
  - `backend/scripts/reports/zeroone-live-test-zeroone-20260408012540525-tstltn.json`.

### What Was Changed

- Reverted Business Center activation test data for member `zeroone` in `charge.registered_members`:
  - deleted the generated replacement primary row (`reg_1775608644331_f971cd61`)
  - restored the original member row (`reg_1775181494655_5d3c5d3e`) from placeholder back to primary
  - rewired impacted downline sponsor reference(s) from `zeroone-bc-1` back to `zeroone`.
- Verified server-side data flush behavior for registered members:
  - admin flush still truncates `charge.registered_members`, which clears all Business Center columns/counters by design.
- Updated flush settings behavior in `admin.html`:
  - expanded member-side browser cleanup list to include newer localStorage keys (Business Center-era claim/commission/trend/profile caches)
  - updated flush confirmation copy to explicitly mention binary-tree and sales-team snapshots
  - added `binaryTreeSnapshots` and `salesTeamCommissions` clear counts to success feedback.

### Design Decisions

- Kept server flush as full-table truncate for canonical reset behavior.
- Added explicit member-browser cache key cleanup on admin flush to reduce stale client-state artifacts after resets.
- Applied DB rollback as a targeted transactional revert for the affected account only (no global flush).

## Update (2026-04-08) - Business Center Binary Tree Feature (Backend + UI + KPI Scope)

### What Was Changed

- Implemented Business Center server-side lifecycle with manual activation, one-at-a-time flow, and side pinning:
  - added authenticated endpoints:
    - `GET /api/member-auth/business-centers`
    - `POST /api/member-auth/business-centers/progress`
    - `POST /api/member-auth/business-centers/activate`
  - mounted Business Center routes in backend app bootstrap.
- Completed Business Center row-model support in registered members storage:
  - owner linkage fields
  - node type/index/label/pinned side
  - legacy completion counter + earned/activated/pending/overflow counters
  - explicit `isStaffTreeAccount` exclusion flag
  - schema migration guard to auto-add missing columns.
- Hardened activation behavior in Business Center service:
  - fixed sponsor rewire edge case that could self-reference placeholder node
  - zeroed placeholder personal-volume fields to avoid double-counting after activation
  - preserved manual side pin behavior and one-step activation sequence.
- Added member dashboard UI controls for Business Center activation in `index.html`:
  - status panel with active/pending/cap/overflow visibility
  - side selector (left/right)
  - activation button + feedback states.
- Wired Legacy Leadership completion progress to server Business Center progress syncing.
- Updated binary tree KPI behavior so Business Center placeholder nodes are excluded from member KPIs:
  - node count
  - new members joined
  - direct sponsors
  - while placeholders remain visible in tree topology.
- Updated tree/tier calculations to treat Business Center placeholders as non-qualifying members for sponsor/tier counting.

### Files Affected

- `backend/app.js`
- `backend/routes/member-business-center.routes.js`
- `backend/controllers/member-business-center.controller.js`
- `backend/services/member-business-center.service.js`
- `backend/services/member.service.js`
- `backend/stores/member.store.js`
- `binary-tree.mjs`
- `index.html`
- `Claude_Notes/binary-tree-business-center.md`
- `Claude_Notes/charge-documentation.md`
- `Claude_Notes/Current Project Status.md`

### Design Decisions

- Business Center placeholders are persisted as `registered_members` rows (no virtual runtime nodes).
- Placeholders remain visible in the binary tree for lineage continuity, but KPI member counts exclude them.
- Activation remains manual and explicit with user-selected legacy-network pin side.
- Legacy completion source is synchronized to canonical server counter via `completedLegacyTierCount`.

### Known Limitations

- Business Center endpoints require member auth bearer token; unauthenticated calls return `401`.
- Existing running dev servers must be restarted to load newly mounted routes.
- Staff/admin exclusion is flag-driven; existing accounts require `isStaffTreeAccount=true` to enforce lockout.

### Validation

- `node --check backend/services/member-business-center.service.js` passed.
- `node --check backend/controllers/member-business-center.controller.js` passed.
- `node --check backend/routes/member-business-center.routes.js` passed.
- `node --check backend/app.js` passed.
- Inline script syntax extracted from `index.html` passed `node --check`.
- Route smoke test on isolated port confirmed auth middleware response:
  - `GET /api/member-auth/business-centers` -> `401 AUTH_REQUIRED` without token.

## Update (2026-04-07) - Unified Member + Free Account Login Flow

### What Was Changed

- Merged login behavior so both paid members and free/preferred customers authenticate from `login.html`.
- Updated post-login routing in `login.html`:
  - paid/member accounts -> `/index.html`
  - free/preferred accounts -> `/store-dashboard.html` (with `?store=` preserved when available).
- Removed free-account rejection behavior from `login.html` (no more forced "Go to Free Member Login" detour).
- Added legacy store-code alias handling in login routing (`CHG-7X42` -> `CHG-ZERO`) to avoid referral-code regressions.
- Converted `store-login.html` into a backward-compatible redirect page that forwards to unified `login.html` while preserving `?store=`.
- Updated shared storefront helper route `buildFreeLoginUrl(...)` to target `/login.html`.
- Updated storefront pages/modals/messages/links that referenced separate free login labels/URLs to the unified login route.
- Updated password setup flows to send both free and paid users to `/login.html`.

### Files Affected

- `login.html`
- `storefront-shared.js`
- `store-login.html`
- `store-dashboard.html`
- `store.html`
- `store-product.html`
- `store-support.html`
- `store-checkout.html`

- `Claude_Notes/Current Project Status.md`
- `Claude_Notes/member-login-page.md`
- `Claude_Notes/preferred-customer-page.md`

### Design / Behavior Decisions

- Kept Admin login untouched (`admin.html` / admin auth path remains separate).
- Preserved legacy `/store-login.html` URL compatibility through redirect instead of hard removal.
- Kept existing account-type checks for destination routing only (not for rejecting authentication).

### Known Limitations

- Public-store login modal still presents two account options, but both unauthenticated flows now converge on `/login.html`.
- `store-login.html` remains as a compatibility shell for older bookmarks and links.

## Update (2026-04-07) - Resize-Locked Background Rendering (No Stretch/Squeeze On Window Resize)

### What Was Changed

- Reworked ColorBends runtime in `login.html` so resize no longer re-scales shader space:
  - introduced fixed internal render resolution:
    - `FIXED_RENDER_WIDTH = 1920`
    - `FIXED_RENDER_HEIGHT = 1080`
  - shader `uCanvas` is now locked to fixed resolution (not resized per viewport).
- Canvas presentation now uses cover behavior for responsive framing:
  - added `object-fit: cover` + `object-position: center center` on background canvas.
- Removed dynamic resize-driven shader resizing/listeners for aspect recalculation.
- Kept pointer math viewport-aware via `getPointerViewportSize()` so interaction still tracks current screen.

### Files Affected

- `login.html`
- `Claude_Notes/charge-documentation.md`
- `Claude_Notes/Current Project Status.md`
- `Claude_Notes/member-login-page.md`

### Validation

- Inline script syntax validation passed for `login.html`.

## Update (2026-04-07) - Mobile Squeeze Follow-Up v3 (Canvas Size Sync + Aspect Clamp)

### What Was Changed

- Applied additional anti-squeeze adjustments in `login.html` ColorBends shader/runtime:
  - shader aspect handling now uses clamped aspect range (`0.72` to `1.4`) to reduce extreme portrait distortion.
  - resize sizing now prioritizes real rendered host bounds via `container.getBoundingClientRect()`.
  - WebGL resize switched to `renderer.setSize(width, height, true)` to force CSS canvas size sync with render buffer dimensions.
- Retained visual-viewport listener approach from v2 while improving primary size source to actual fixed background host rect.

### Files Affected

- `login.html`
- `Claude_Notes/charge-documentation.md`
- `Claude_Notes/Current Project Status.md`
- `Claude_Notes/member-login-page.md`

## Update (2026-04-07) - Mobile Squeeze Follow-Up Fix (Viewport-Fixed Background)

### What Was Changed

- Applied a deeper mobile background fix in `login.html` after initial aspect correction was still insufficient.
- Switched ColorBends background host from document-relative absolute positioning to viewport-fixed positioning:
  - `#color-bends-bg` now uses `fixed inset-0` so it always matches the visual viewport rather than growing with page content height.
- Updated shader resize plumbing to use viewport-aware dimensions:
  - added `getViewportSize()` with `window.visualViewport` fallback chain
  - resize updates now bind to:
    - `window.resize`
    - `window.orientationchange`
    - `visualViewport.resize`
    - `visualViewport.scroll`.
- Pointer normalization now uses the same viewport-aware dimensions to keep interaction aligned on mobile.

### Files Affected

- `login.html`
- `Claude_Notes/charge-documentation.md`
- `Claude_Notes/Current Project Status.md`
- `Claude_Notes/member-login-page.md`

## Update (2026-04-07) - Mobile ColorBends Squeeze Fix (Portrait Aspect)

### What Was Changed

- Corrected ColorBends shader aspect mapping in `login.html` to prevent compressed background appearance on mobile portrait layouts.
- Replaced single-axis aspect scale:
  - previous: `q.x *= (uCanvas.x / uCanvas.y)`
  - updated: conditional cover-style mapping:
    - wide screens (`aspect > 1`): scale `q.x`
    - tall screens (`aspect <= 1`): scale `q.y` inversely.

- `login.html`

  - added `deviceScaleFactor` default `2`
  - result default capture output is now `7680x4320` (8K UHD) when using the screenshot script.
- Added env override support for capture resolution in `screenshot.mjs`:
  - `SCREENSHOT_WIDTH`

  - `SCREENSHOT_DSF`
- Increased ColorBends canvas render resolution in `login.html`:
  - replaced capped pixel ratio (`min(..., 2)`) with uncapped device ratio (`max(1, devicePixelRatio)`).

## Update (2026-04-07) - Color Bends Converted to Custom JS/CSS Only (No Video Layer)

### What Was Changed

- Removed the Color Bends background video layer from `login.html`.
- Installed `three` (`npm install three`) and switched the login background runtime to local module import:
  - `import * as THREE from '/node_modules/three/build/three.module.js'`
  - removed broken CDN reference that was returning `404` and preventing shader startup.
- Added a single `colorBendsConfig` object in `login.html` that mirrors the Usage API fields, so customization now maps 1:1 to provided props.
- Kept the background implementation fully generated by:
  - live Three.js shader (Color Bends fragment/vertex logic)
  - CSS gradient fallback bends behind the shader for non-WebGL/headless cases.
- Retained black background composition and dark glassmorphism login surface.
- Preserved provided Color Bends prop profile values in shader setup (`colors`, `rotation`, `speed`, `scale`, `frequency`, `warpStrength`, `mouseInfluence`, `parallax`, `noise`).

### Files Affected

- `login.html`
- `Claude_Notes/charge-documentation.md`
- `Claude_Notes/Current Project Status.md`
- `Claude_Notes/member-login-page.md`

### Design Decisions

- Removed media playback dependency so the effect reads as custom-generated rather than video playback.
- Added CSS fallback intentionally to keep the intended bend composition visible when WebGL output is constrained (e.g., some headless screenshot environments).

- Applied the provided Color Bends parameter profile in implementation:
  - `colors: ["#ff5c7a", "#8a5cff", "#00ffd1"]`
  - `rotation: 0`
  - `speed: 0.2`
  - `scale: 1`
  - `frequency: 1`
  - `warpStrength: 1`
  - `mouseInfluence: 1`
  - `parallax: 0.5`
  - `noise: 0.1`
  - `transparent: true`
  - `autoRotate: 0`
- Shifted page visual mode back to dark (`color-scheme: dark`) so the login screen background reads as black-first.
- Kept the login card in a dark glassmorphism style so the content remains readable over bright bends.
- Added the official Color Bends media layer as a visual base and kept shader overlay active to preserve live behavior and close screenshot matching.

### Files Affected

- `login.html`
- `Claude_Notes/charge-documentation.md`
- `Claude_Notes/Current Project Status.md`
- `Claude_Notes/member-login-page.md`

### Design Decisions

- Prioritized visual alignment to the provided screenshot (black base and neon arc composition) over the previous light-theme login pass.
- Preserved existing login/auth IDs and JS auth flow; changes remained presentation-focused.

## Update (2026-04-07) - Member Login Light Theme + Color Bends Glassmorph

### What Was Changed

- Restyled `login.html` from dark mode to a light-first default (`<meta name="color-scheme" content="light">`).
- Replaced the previous static login backdrop with a ReactBits-inspired `Color Bends` treatment:
  - added animated shader canvas (Three.js) based on the Color Bends fragment/vertex flow
  - added official Color Bends fallback media layer sourced from ReactBits assets for visual continuity.
- Rebuilt the login card shell into a glassmorphism surface:
  - translucent panel (`bg-white` alpha), heavy backdrop blur, soft highlight overlay, and tinted layered shadows
  - preserved existing login form IDs and auth wiring (`#login-form`, `#login-identifier`, `#login-password`, `#login-submit`).
- Updated typography and color tokens to match the light theme while preserving brand-purple direction.

- `login.html`
- `Claude_Notes/charge-documentation.md`
- `Claude_Notes/Current Project Status.md`
- `Claude_Notes/member-login-page.md`

- Used brand logo asset (`/brand_assets/Logos/Premiere Life Logo_Transparent.svg`) instead of placeholder branding.
- Kept the auth logic untouched and scoped work to visual/theming layer only.
- Added progressive fallback for background rendering (video + shader) so the page still visually matches the Color Bends request when WebGL support varies.

## Update (2026-04-07) - Account Overview Main Metric Graph Added (Total vs Personal BV)

### What Was Changed

- Added a comparison chart to the left/main Account Overview panel (`Weekly Total Organization BV`) in `index.html`.
- Chart behavior follows the same interaction style as the Personal Volume KPI graph:
  - animated bar reveal using existing `.dashboard-pv-data-bar` animation class
  - hover/focus tooltip
  - compact caption summarizing trend change.
- Implemented dual-series daily comparison:
  - series 1: `Total Organization BV` (darker bar)
  - series 2: `Weekly Personal Organization BV` (lighter bar)
  - rendered as two bars per day for a 30-day timeline.
- Added new Account Overview BV trend persistence/state pipeline:
  - storage key: `charge-account-overview-bv-trend-v1`
  - per-user partitioning via normalized user key
  - daily dedupe logic (latest entry per day retained)
  - 30-day series builder + tooltip renderer + caption builder.
- Wired graph updates into existing dashboard summary lifecycle in `applyBinaryTreeDashboardSummary(...)`:
  - compares `totalAccumulatedPv` against `organizationRollups.personalOrganizationBv`.

### Files Affected

- `index.html`
- `Claude_Notes/charge-documentation.md`
- `Claude_Notes/Current Project Status.md`

### Design Decisions

- Reused the Personal Volume chart animation system to keep interaction and motion consistent across dashboard KPI visuals.
- Kept existing text/trend IDs untouched so existing metric updates continue to work without regressions.
- Used two contrast levels of brand color in-chart plus an explicit legend for quick comparison readability.

### Validation

- Inline script syntax validation passed for `index.html`.

## Update (2026-04-07) - Dashboard Account Overview Refactor (Rank Card Removed + Fast Track Separated)

### What Was Changed

- Refactored the Dashboard `Account Overview` block in `index.html`:
  - removed the previous `Account Rank` panel from the Account Overview component.
  - reorganized overview metrics into a clean 4-card grid.
- Metrics now shown in Account Overview:
  - `Weekly Total Organization BV`
  - `New Members Joined Your Network`
  - `Total Direct Sponsors`
  - `Cycles` (new) using existing runtime binding `#dashboard-total-cycles-value`.
- Separated `Fast Track Bonus` into its own container:
  - moved from inside the Account Overview card to a dedicated sibling container below it in the left dashboard column.
  - retained all existing Fast Track IDs and transfer button behavior.

### Files Affected

- `index.html`
- `Claude_Notes/charge-documentation.md`
- `Claude_Notes/Current Project Status.md`

### Design Decisions

- Preserved existing metric/trend IDs used by runtime update functions to avoid data-binding regressions.
- Kept `account-overview-card` and rank dataset metadata (`data-account-rank`) in place for compatibility with existing rank-related logic.
- Introduced `Cycles` as a first-class overview metric so cycle visibility is now within the same summary cluster requested by UX.

### Known Limitations

- Account rank upgrade controls remain in script logic but are no longer exposed in the Dashboard Account Overview UI after this pass.

### Validation

- Inline script syntax validation passed for `index.html` after markup changes.

## Update (2026-04-07) - E-Wallet KPI Card: Single Action + 30-Day Balance Trend

### What Was Changed

- Updated the Home dashboard `E-Wallet Balance` KPI card in `index.html` to match the latest UX request:
  - removed the two existing CTA buttons (`Send`, `Transfer`)
  - added one CTA only: `Go to E-Wallet`
  - CTA now opens the `e-wallet` page view via `setPage('e-wallet', { routeMode: 'push' })`.
- Added a 30-day balance graph block directly in the E-Wallet KPI card:
  - `#dashboard-ewallet-balance-bars`
  - `#dashboard-ewallet-balance-tooltip`
  - `#dashboard-ewallet-balance-tooltip-date`
  - `#dashboard-ewallet-balance-tooltip-value`
  - `#dashboard-ewallet-balance-graph-caption`
- Implemented E-Wallet trend state + renderer in dashboard script:
  - persistent trend key: `charge-dashboard-ewallet-trend-v1`
  - per-user trend partitioning with normalized member key
  - daily bucket dedupe (latest server timestamp per day wins)
  - 30-day daily series render with tooltip and animated bar reveal (aligned with Personal Volume graph behavior).
- Wired KPI graph refresh into E-Wallet summary lifecycle:
  - `renderEWalletSummary()` now updates E-Wallet KPI trend each time wallet snapshot data is rendered.

### Files Affected

- `index.html`
- `Claude_Notes/charge-documentation.md`
- `Claude_Notes/Current Project Status.md`

### Design Decisions

- Kept graph interaction parity with Personal Volume (compact bars, hover/focus tooltip, dynamic caption) so KPI cards feel consistent.
- Kept CTA behavior explicit and navigation-first (`Go to E-Wallet`) to reduce action ambiguity at KPI level.
- Used server-confirmed wallet snapshot timestamps for trend writes and avoided synthetic historical reconstruction.

### Known Limitations

- Historical day values are accumulated over time from observed server snapshots; this pass does not retroactively rebuild all prior days from transfer history.

---

## Update (2026-04-07) - Account Active Until KPI Badge Icons Added (Reuse Profile Badges)

### What Was Changed

- Enhanced the `Account Active Until` KPI card in `index.html` to reduce empty space in the bottom area by adding a 3-icon badge strip.
- Added badges for:
  - Rank Icon
  - Title 1 Icon
  - Title 2 Icon
- Reused the existing Profile badge data pipeline instead of duplicating logic:
  - reads from `resolveProfileHandleBadgeEntries(...)`
  - keeps icons in sync with Profile badge state and visibility.
- Added fallback behavior:
  - when Title 1/Title 2 are not available yet, placeholders are shown so the row remains visually balanced.

### Files Affected

- `index.html`
- `Claude_Notes/charge-documentation.md`
- `Claude_Notes/Current Project Status.md`

### Design Decisions

- Introduced a dedicated KPI badge shell style (`dashboard-account-kpi-*`) that visually matches Profile badge treatment while remaining non-interactive.
- Rendered exactly three slots in KPI for stable layout rhythm (Rank, Title 1, Title 2).

### Validation

- Headless DOM check confirmed:
  - KPI badge strip exists
  - exactly 3 badge items render
  - icon sources are populated from profile-derived entries (with placeholders for locked titles).

### Known Limitations

- KPI badge strip is icon-only and non-interactive (no hover card); detailed badge context remains in the Profile page badge hovercard.

## Update (2026-04-07) - Sidebar Layout Regression Fix + Sidebar State Persistence

### What Was Changed

- Fixed a structural HTML regression in `index.html` that caused these page sections to render outside the shared `main`/`lg:ml-64` dashboard wrapper:
  - `Profile`
  - `My Store`
  - `Enroll Member`
  - `Preferred Customers`
  - `Binary Tree`
- Root cause was an extra closing `</div>` near the end of the `E-Wallet` section, which broke expected DOM nesting and made affected pages ignore sidebar offset behavior.
- Added desktop sidebar collapse persistence across reloads by extending existing dashboard view-state storage:
  - stores `sidebarCollapsed` in `charge-dashboard-view-state`
  - restores collapsed/open state on initialization before `setPage(...)`
  - keeps state synced when sidebar is collapsed/opened on desktop

### Files Affected

- `index.html`
- `Claude_Notes/charge-documentation.md`
- `Claude_Notes/Current Project Status.md`

### Design Decisions

- Kept sidebar persistence inside the existing view-state storage key instead of introducing a second localStorage key to avoid fragmented UI state.
- Limited persistence to desktop collapsed/open behavior (`data-sidebar-collapsed`) to preserve expected mobile overlay navigation behavior.

### Validation

- DOM structure verification (headless browser) confirms all page views now mount inside `main` and the shared wrapper:
  - `page-dashboard`, `page-profile`, `page-enroll-member`, `page-preferred-customer`, `page-binary-tree`, `page-my-store`, `page-e-wallet`, `page-settings`
- Sidebar persistence verification (headless browser):
  - collapse -> reload remains collapsed
  - reopen -> reload remains open
  - wrapper margin-left transitions as expected (`0px` collapsed, `256px` open on current desktop shell)

### Known Limitations

- Mobile sidebar overlay open/closed state is not persisted by this pass; only desktop collapsed/open state is persisted.

---

## Update (2026-04-07) - KPI Card Rework (E-Wallet + Account Overview Swap)

### What Was Changed

- Updated Home KPI cards in `index.html` based on focused UX feedback:
  - Reframed `Total Balance` KPI as `E-Wallet Balance` (same data binding/ID, clearer card intent).
  - Kept `Personal Volume` KPI.
  - Removed `Cycles` KPI card from the top KPI strip.
  - Moved `Sales Team Commissions` into the former cycles position.
  - Removed visible `Per cycle` and `Weekly cap` text row from the Sales Team Commissions KPI to reduce clutter.
  - Added a new `Account Overview` KPI card showing:
    - account activity state (`Active`/`Inactive`)
    - remaining/elapsed activity window
    - current rank label
- Added KPI-specific bindings and render updates:
  - new DOM refs for account overview KPI status/rank/time
  - new renderer `renderDashboardAccountOverviewKpi(...)`
  - activity/rank rendering now syncs both the existing account section and the new KPI card.
- Removed decorative bottom filler styling from KPI cards to eliminate the ????????????????graph-like empty space?????? feel.

### Files Affected

- `index.html`
- `Claude_Notes/charge-documentation.md`
- `Claude_Notes/Current Project Status.md`

### Design Decisions

- Prioritized primary values and immediate readability over secondary explanatory rows.
- Kept existing logic-sensitive IDs and business hooks intact where possible, with additive KPI-specific IDs for the new Account Overview card.
- Preserved Sales Team cap/progression logic while simplifying visible KPI copy.

### Validation

- Authenticated screenshot passes captured:
  - `temporary screenshots/screenshot-89-kpi-rework-request-pass1.png`
  - `temporary screenshots/screenshot-90-kpi-rework-request-pass2-crop.png`
- Confirmed KPI strip now reflects:
  - E-Wallet Balance
  - Personal Volume
  - Sales Team Commissions (without visible per-cycle/weekly-cap text row)
  - Account Overview (status + active window + rank)

### Known Limitations

- `Sales Team Commissions` still references cycle-cap usage summary/progress (by design) even though the dedicated Cycles KPI card was removed.
- This pass is focused on dark-mode visual verification; a dedicated light-mode check can be done next.

## Update (2026-04-07) - Home Dashboard KPI + Account Overview Declutter Pass

### What Was Changed

- Redesigned the top `Home` KPI strip in `index.html` to reduce visual fatigue and card clutter while preserving all existing KPI data IDs and payout actions.
- Reworked KPI presentation for:
  - `Total Balance`
  - `Personal Volume`
  - `Cycles`
  - `Sales Team Commissions`
- Shifted KPI layout behavior to reduce compression:
  - standard desktop keeps a 2-column KPI rhythm
  - very wide desktop (`2xl`) uses 4 columns
- Redesigned `Account Overview` into a calmer two-pane composition:
  - left pane: rank + status + upgrade controls
  - right pane: simplified metric rows for weekly BV, new members, and direct sponsors
- Kept logic-sensitive bonus modules untouched (structure and JS hooks preserved):
  - Fast Track Bonus
  - Infinity Tier Commission
  - Legacy Leadership Bonus

### Files Affected

- `index.html`
- `Claude_Notes/charge-documentation.md`
- `Claude_Notes/Current Project Status.md`

### Design Decisions

- Prioritized readability and scan flow over dense card count to address user-reported eye fatigue.
- Preserved all existing IDs tied to dashboard JS/render flows to avoid behavior regressions.
- Used subtle brand-tinted glows, quieter typography hierarchy, and reduced nested surfaces to align with Apple/Shopify-inspired clarity.

### Validation

- Captured authenticated dashboard screenshots after implementation and refinement:
  - `temporary screenshots/screenshot-86-kpi-overview-redesign-pass1.png`
  - `temporary screenshots/screenshot-87-kpi-overview-redesign-pass2.png`
  - `temporary screenshots/screenshot-88-kpi-overview-redesign-pass2-crop.png`
- Confirmed KPI and Account Overview redesign render while preserving Fast Track, Infinity Builder, and Legacy Leadership module structure.

### Known Limitations

- This pass focuses on KPI + Account Overview composition only; the right-column utility cards and deeper module-level typography harmonization can be refined in a follow-up.
- Light-mode-only visual QA for this exact redesign was not completed in this pass.

  - dark/default/shopify themes keep dark translucent sidebar
  - light/apple themes now use light surface-based sidebar background

  - increased logo shell max width and height allowance
  - removed clipping behavior (`overflow: visible`)
  - constrained logo by `max-height` with `object-fit: contain` for clean scaling

- Kept 21st-style dark sidebar treatment intact for dark-mode themes only.
- Scoped the fix to theme-aware selectors so light mode can follow light token surfaces consistently.
- Preferred CSS-level corrections over markup rewrites to minimize risk to existing navigation logic.

### Validation

- Captured and reviewed light-mode screenshot after applying local `appTheme='light'`.
- Confirmed sidebar background now follows light theme and logo no longer appears clipped.

- Applied a new dark theme token system in `:root`:
  - near-black neutral surfaces
  - subtle border hierarchy
  - muted cool-blue accent ramp
  - reduced glow-heavy styling
- Sidebar structure and rhythm were updated:
  - tighter spacing
  - added sidebar search field with `????????????? K` hint
  - added section labels (`Explore`, `Build`)
  - adjusted active nav styling to neutral 21st-like state (less saturated highlight)
  - renamed dashboard nav label to `Home`
- Top shell and content presentation were tuned:
  - refined top bar density/blur/border treatment
  - centered page title behavior on larger screens
  - hid top-bar search to match the simpler 21st top layout feel
  - tightened main content spacing and card corner radii
  - flattened heavy gradient/glow card treatments in dashboard sections
- Updated dark-mode logo treatment to a mono-white logo variant for a cleaner sidebar header look.

### Files Affected

- `index.html`
- `Claude_Notes/charge-documentation.md`
- `Claude_Notes/Current Project Status.md`

### Design Decisions

- Prioritized matching visual mood and layout rhythm (not full content parity) so existing dashboard behavior remains intact.
- Kept all routing/data interactions in place while making the shell look and feel closer to 21st.
- Used token-level dark palette changes plus targeted layout CSS overrides to produce broad visual alignment without rewriting page logic.

### Validation

- Captured and reviewed live reference screenshot from `https://21st.dev/home`.
- Confirmed dark token block, sidebar structure, and page-meta home title updates were applied in `index.html`.

### Known Limitations

- This pass focuses on shell feel and dashboard styling; it does not replicate every exact component/content pattern from 21st home.
- Additional iteration may be needed for tighter one-to-one parity in card-level spacing and typography.

## Update (2026-04-06) - Light Mode Restyled To Apple-Inspired Palette

### What Was Changed

- Replaced the entire `html[data-theme='light']` color token set in `index.html` to shift from the prior lavender/emerald styling to an Apple-like light system.
- New light mode direction:
  - cool neutral surfaces (`#F5F5F7` style base)
  - clean white raised/elevated layers
  - dark graphite typography hierarchy
  - blue accent ramp centered on Apple-like action blue (`#0071E3`)
- Updated token groups in light mode:
  - `--brand-50` to `--brand-950`
  - `--surface-*`
  - `--text-*`
  - `--semantic-*`
  - `--shadow-*`
  - `--theme-page-gradient`, `--theme-overlay*`, minimap tokens
  - selection and scrollbar tokens
- Restored light-mode `--text-inverse` to white for improved contrast on blue primary action buttons.

### Files Affected

- `index.html`
- `Claude_Notes/charge-documentation.md`
- `Claude_Notes/Current Project Status.md`

### Design Decisions

- Kept dark mode unchanged and scoped this pass to light-mode identity only.
- Used an intentionally restrained Apple-style system: soft grays + subtle blue accents instead of saturated multi-color gradients.
- Maintained accessibility-minded contrast for key button, text, and accent states used throughout the dashboard.

### Validation

- Confirmed full replacement of the `html[data-theme='light']` token block in `index.html`.
- Performed quick contrast spot-checks for core combinations:
  - white text on `--brand-500`
  - primary/secondary text on surface base

### Known Limitations

- This was a global token pass; component-by-component visual polishing (if needed) should be done in a dedicated light-mode QA sweep.

## Update (2026-04-06) - Light Mode Palette Realignment (Brand-Consistent)

### What Was Changed

- Replaced the `html[data-theme='light']` token set in `index.html` to align light mode with brand direction.
- Shifted the primary light palette from lavender-heavy to emerald-led values based on available brand assets:
  - green anchor from logo assets (`#67B392`)
  - purple preserved as semantic accent (`#7853A2`)
- Updated token groups:
  - `--brand-50` to `--brand-950`
  - surface tokens (`--surface-*`)
  - text tokens (`--text-*`)
  - semantic tokens (`--semantic-*`)
  - light-theme overlay/minimap/selection/scrollbar tokens
- Set `--text-inverse` for light mode to a dark ink value so text remains readable on lighter green action buttons.

### Files Affected

- `index.html`
- `Claude_Notes/charge-documentation.md`
- `Claude_Notes/Current Project Status.md`

### Design Decisions

- Kept dark mode untouched and focused this pass only on light-mode color fidelity.
- Chose a soft off-white + green-neutral surface system to better match the logo family and reduce the previous cross-theme mismatch.
- Kept purple in the system as an accent semantic/info color to preserve brand flexibility without making it the light-mode base.

### Validation

### Known Limitations

## Update (2026-04-06) - Notification Center Mobile Layout Fix

### What Was Changed

- Improved notification center behavior and spacing for mobile view in `index.html`.
- Notification panel now behaves as a mobile sheet:
  - `fixed` positioning on small screens
  - viewport-safe top offset using safe-area inset
  - mobile max-height tied to viewport so content remains accessible
  - desktop behavior preserved with `sm:` overrides
- Notification list now uses a responsive max-height (`mobile viewport-based`, `desktop fixed`).
- Header control crowding on mobile reduced:
  - tightened right-side spacing
  - reduced profile button padding on small screens
  - hid top-bar logout button on mobile (`hidden sm:inline-flex`)
- Added mobile scroll lock when notification sheet is open (`body overflow-hidden` on mobile only).

### Files Affected

- `index.html`
- `Claude_Notes/charge-documentation.md`
- `Claude_Notes/Current Project Status.md`

### Design Decisions

- Prioritized usable viewport space and predictable panel behavior on phones over desktop-style dropdown behavior.
- Kept desktop notification experience unchanged while using responsive overrides to improve small-screen ergonomics.

### Validation

- Frontend inline script parse check passed:
  - `inline_scripts_checked=2`

### Known Limitations

- This fix targets notification center mobile UX only; broader header layout refinements can be done in a separate pass if needed.

## Update (2026-04-06) - Member Notification Center + Announcement System

### What Was Changed

- Implemented a new authenticated member notification system with announcement support.
- Added backend notification APIs under member-auth:
  - `GET /api/member-auth/notifications`
  - `POST /api/member-auth/notifications/:notificationId/read`
  - `POST /api/member-auth/notifications/mark-all-read`
- Added server-side storage for:
  - notifications/announcements records
  - per-member read tracking
  - seed data for initial notifications and announcements
- Replaced static header bell in `index.html` with an interactive Notification Center panel:
  - tabbed views (`Notifications`, `Announcements`)
  - unread badges
  - mark single item read
  - mark active tab items read
  - CTA routing from notification cards
- Added client initialization + event wiring for open/close behavior, outside-click close, escape close, and authenticated API sync.

### Files Affected

- `index.html`
- `backend/app.js`
- `backend/routes/member-notification.routes.js`
- `backend/controllers/member-notification.controller.js`
- `backend/services/member-notification.service.js`
- `backend/stores/member-notification.store.js`
- `Claude_Notes/charge-documentation.md`
- `Claude_Notes/Current Project Status.md`

### Design Decisions

- Kept notifications and announcements in one API payload for simpler client hydration and a single header fetch path.
- Persisted read-state server-side by member user id so unread counts are stable across sessions/devices.
- Seeded default system entries so the notification center has meaningful first-run content without requiring admin setup.
- Scoped this pass to member consumption and read-state actions; no admin creation/editor UI was added in this iteration.

### Validation

- Syntax checks passed:
  - `node --check backend/stores/member-notification.store.js`
  - `node --check backend/services/member-notification.service.js`
  - `node --check backend/controllers/member-notification.controller.js`
  - `node --check backend/routes/member-notification.routes.js`
  - `node --check backend/app.js`
- Escalated local smoke checks passed:
  - `GET /api/health` -> `200`
  - `GET /api/member-auth/notifications` without token -> `401 AUTH_REQUIRED`
  - `POST /api/member-auth/notifications/:notificationId/read` without token -> `401 AUTH_REQUIRED`
  - `POST /api/member-auth/notifications/mark-all-read` without token -> `401 AUTH_REQUIRED`
- Served `index.html` confirms notification center markup IDs and tab controls are present in output.

### Known Limitations

- Admin CRUD for creating/editing announcements is not included yet (current content is seed-driven).
- Authenticated end-to-end read-flow validation depends on a live member token session and was not executed in this pass.

---

## Update (2026-04-06) - Product Media Interaction Lock (No Drag / No Highlight)

### What Was Changed

- Added interaction lock rules on product media so images are not draggable and do not allow selection highlight behavior.
- Applied on storefront, dashboard store view, and admin product media previews/lists.
- Product media now consistently includes:
  - `draggable="false"`
  - `select-none`
  - inline `-webkit-user-drag: none;`

### Files Affected

- `store.html`
- `store-product.html`
- `index.html`
- `admin.html`
- `Claude_Notes/charge-documentation.md`
- `Claude_Notes/Current Project Status.md`
- `Claude_Notes/public-store-page.md`

### Design Decisions

- Scoped this interaction lock to product-media image elements to avoid unexpected behavior changes in unrelated UI/media.
- Kept existing hover/scale transitions and button/card interactions unchanged.

### Validation

- Code audit check confirms product-media render paths now carry non-drag/non-select attributes:
  - public store grid image
  - product detail hero image
  - product detail thumbnail gallery images
  - dashboard store grid + selected product image
  - admin product preview and admin product list thumbnail

### Known Limitations

- This update targets product media only; non-product image assets in other modules were intentionally left untouched.

## Update (2026-04-06) - Product Media Ratio Standardized To 4:5

### What Was Changed

- Updated product media presentation from landscape to portrait `4:5` across storefront and admin product surfaces.
- Updated product image placeholders/defaults to a `4:5` asset size (`1000x1250`) so fallback media matches the new ratio.
- Updated admin product media guidance text to recommend `1600x2000 (4:5)` uploads.

### Files Affected

- `store.html`
- `store-product.html`
- `index.html`
- `admin.html`
- `storefront-shared.js`
- `backend/services/store-product.service.js`
- `backend/stores/store-product.store.js`
- `Claude_Notes/charge-documentation.md`
- `Claude_Notes/Current Project Status.md`
- `Claude_Notes/public-store-page.md`

### Design Decisions

- Applied `aspect-[4/5]` directly on product image elements for consistent ratio behavior across grid cards, detail views, thumbnails, and admin previews.
- Kept `object-cover` so existing product photos still fill frames cleanly without layout shifts.
- Standardized fallback image dimensions to prevent mixed aspect-ratio rendering when product media is missing.

### Validation

- Syntax checks passed:
  - `node --check storefront-shared.js`
  - `node --check backend/services/store-product.service.js`
  - `node --check backend/stores/store-product.store.js`
