## Update (2026-04-16) - Preferred Panel Resize Stability (Prevent Canvas Blackout)

### What Was Changed

- Added a guarded Preferred Accounts sync block inside the main render loop.
- Preferred panel position/visual/visibility sync now runs inside `try/catch` during frame render.
- Added throttled console error logging (`>=1000ms`) for Preferred panel sync failures during render.

### Why

- A runtime exception in Preferred panel sync during resize could stop the render loop, leaving only DOM overlays visible and the tree canvas appearing black.
- Guarding this path keeps core tree rendering alive during resize even if panel sync hits transient errors.

### Files Affected

- binary-tree-next-app.mjs
- Claude_Notes/charge-documentation.md
- Claude_Notes/Current Project Status.md
- Claude_Notes/binary-tree-next.md

### Validation

- node --check binary-tree-next-app.mjs passed.

## Update (2026-04-16) - Preferred Panel Drag Freeze Fix (Render Loop Crash Root Cause)

### What Was Changed

- Fixed a render-loop crash in `syncAccountOverviewPanelVisuals()` when Preferred Customers is visible:
  - replaced invalid `options?.force` reference with a safe `maybeRefreshPreferredAccountsSnapshot()` call.
- Added render-loop crash recovery guard in `tickFrame()`:
  - wrapped per-frame render/update body in `try/catch`
  - added throttled error logging (`>=1000ms`)
  - `requestAnimationFrame(tickFrame)` now runs from `finally` so one frame error cannot permanently stop rendering.

### Root Cause

- `syncAccountOverviewPanelVisuals()` referenced `options` that does not exist in that scope.
- When Preferred panel was open, this threw a `ReferenceError`, killing the RAF loop.
- Once RAF stopped, the tree appeared frozen/black and drag interactions appeared broken even after closing the panel.

### Files Affected

- binary-tree-next-app.mjs
- Claude_Notes/charge-documentation.md
- Claude_Notes/Current Project Status.md
- Claude_Notes/binary-tree-next.md

### Validation

- node --check binary-tree-next-app.mjs passed.

## Update (2026-04-16) - Preferred Placement Save UX/Latency Improvement

### What Was Changed

- Reduced perceived save delay for Preferred placement updates:
  - removed blocking `await refreshPreferredAccountsSnapshot({ force: true })` from the successful save path
  - now refreshes Preferred snapshot in background after save success.
- Added save progress feedback:
  - shows `Saving profile plan...` while API request is in-flight.
- Added save loading animation:
  - Save button now enters `is-loading` state with spinner animation and `aria-busy` updates.
- Added clearer success note:
  - after successful save, shows `Profile plan saved successfully.`
  - success state has dedicated styling (`is-success`).
- Preserved immediate UI consistency by updating selected row placement option optimistically after success, then background-refreshing from server.

### Why It Felt Slow

- The previous implementation waited for a full forced Preferred panel refresh (members + invoices) before completing the save cycle and returning the button to normal.

## Update (2026-04-16) - My Store in User Dashboard Reused Latest `store.html` Flow

### What Was Changed

- Replaced the visible legacy `My Store` storefront experience in `index.html` with an embedded latest-store shell.
- Added a storefront embed container and iframe in dashboard `My Store` view:
  - iframe source now points to `/store.html?embedded=1`
  - includes an `Open Full Store` action for full-page access.
- Preserved the previous dashboard storefront flow by wrapping it in a hidden legacy container (`#storefront-legacy-flow`) for rollback safety.
- Added iframe sync logic in dashboard app script:
  - introduced `syncStorefrontEmbedFrame(...)`
  - appends active store code (`store=...`) to iframe URL
  - avoids unnecessary reloads using a `data-last-src` guard
  - runs during My Store init and whenever storefront tab becomes active.
- Added embedded-display mode support to `store.html`:
  - `embedded=1` query activates `data-store-embedded`
  - top navigation is hidden in embedded mode
  - page/hero spacing tightened for dashboard framing.

### Files Affected

- `index.html`
- `store.html`
- `Claude_Notes/member-dashboard-page.md`
- `Claude_Notes/public-store-page.md`
- `Claude_Notes/charge-documentation.md`
- `Claude_Notes/Current Project Status.md`

### Design Decisions

- Reused the canonical `store.html` flow directly instead of duplicating UI/state inside dashboard.
- Kept legacy dashboard store markup in-place but hidden to allow fast fallback during transition.

### Known Limitations

- Embedded viewport currently uses a fixed iframe height (`1200px`); dynamic auto-resize can be added in a follow-up.

## Update (2026-04-16) - My Store Warm-Load While on Home + First-Open Stretch Reduction

  - preloads top storefront product images in background.
- Integrated warm-preload into `initMyStore()` right after catalog load.

  - waits briefly (`Promise.race` with ~320ms cap) so first open has assets hydrated without blocking the dashboard.
- Moved `void initMyStore();` earlier in startup sequence so My Store warm-init starts sooner while user is on Home.

  - new left profile dock action `panel:rank-advancement:toggle`

  - opening Rank Advancement closes Account Overview, Preferred Accounts, and My Store
  - opening Preferred Accounts or My Store now closes Rank Advancement.

### Addendum (2026-04-16) - Member-Rank Requirement Enforcement + Rank Tap Latency Fix

- Fixed rank advancement gating mismatch where higher ranks could appear reached without required lower-rank members in-network.
- Added frontend member-rank requirement enforcement for rank milestones using network descendant rank counts:
  - Emerald requires 1 Ruby member
  - Sapphire requires 1 Emerald member
  - Diamond requires 1 Sapphire member
  - Blue Diamond requires 1 Diamond member
  - Black Diamond requires 1 Blue Diamond member
  - Crown requires 2 Black Diamond members
  - Double Crown requires 2 Crown members
  - Royal Crown requires 2 Double Crown members.
- Updated earned-rank and claimable-rank calculations to honor member-rank requirement gating before displaying/rewarding higher rank states.
- Requirement copy cleanup:
  - removed extra verbose progress suffix text from Personal BV and Direct Sponsor lines
  - met-state now indicated via green color + checkmark icon.
- Fixed rank icon preview delay:
  - milestone click now rebuilds rank-advancement snapshot immediately from cached payloads (no waiting for next sync interval).

## Update (2026-04-16) - Binary Tree Next Infinity Tier Commission Panel UI (New)

### What Was Changed

- Implemented a new Infinity Tier Commission panel in `binary-tree-next.html` and `binary-tree-next-app.mjs`.

  - state flag (`infinityBuilderVisible`)
  - position/visibility/render sync
  - close + initialization behavior.

  - clicking `Infinity Tier Commission` now opens the Infinity panel.

  - Account Overview
  - Rank Advancement
  - Preferred Accounts
  - My Store
  and vice versa.
- Added query-based panel deep link support:
  - `?panel=infinity-builder`
  - plus sibling keys for existing panels.
- Added real-data Infinity tier UI rendering from Binary Tree Next live nodes:
  - seed qualification: Infinity/Legacy package enrollments
  - `Current` list sorted oldest-to-new
  - clickable tier rows
  - selected tier trinary node section (3 seed nodes x 3 child nodes)
  - initials rendered for profile nodes
  - active/inactive gate coloring via `resolveNodeActivityState(...)`
  - gradient styling via existing Binary Tree Next avatar palette logic.
- Added tier summary behavior:
  - per-seed 1% override estimate from node organization BV
  - tier completion state (3 completed nodes)
  - tier reward display (`$150.00 USD`)
  - claim-state read from commission-container claim map when available.

### Addendum (2026-04-17) - Legacy Trinary Depth-Sizing Parity With Binary Rendering

Summary:
- Upgraded Legacy Leadership trinary canvas node sizing to follow Binary Tree Next depth-scaling behavior instead of the previous generic fixed ladder.

  - `LEGACY_TIER_CANVAS_RADIUS_DEPTH_DECAY = 0.56`
  - `LEGACY_TIER_CANVAS_WORLD_RADIUS_MIN = 0.0002`
- Refactored `resolveLegacyTierCanvasFrame(...)` radius generation:
  - depth radius now derives from Binary-style decay (`NODE_RADIUS_BASE * decay^depth`),
  - per-depth viewport slot limits clamp oversized circles on smaller widths,
  - projected radius remains camera-driven through existing world-space projection math.
- Result: root/level radii now separate more clearly by depth and behave consistently under zoom like Binary view.

### Addendum (2026-04-17) - Legacy Trinary Connector Alignment + Length Pass

Summary:
- Fixed crooked Legacy trinary connector geometry by changing node placement from row-slot spacing to parent-centered trinary branching.
- Shortened perceived connector lengths by tightening Legacy depth Y spacing.

Technical details:
- Updated `resolveLegacyTierCanvasFrame(...)` in `binary-tree-next-app.mjs`:
  - Replaced per-row `slotStep` X placement with trinary branch-path placement (`left/middle/right`) around each parent.
  - Added deterministic per-node trinary path generation using parent sibling order.
  - Added geometric branch-step decay so each depth fans out symmetrically while remaining centered.
  - Added depth-ratio based Y placement (`[0.04, 0.30, 0.53, 0.73]`) to compress vertical spacing and reduce long connector runs.
- Existing connector drawing pipeline remains unchanged; geometry correction is driven by corrected node coordinates.

### Addendum (2026-04-17) - Header Behavior Split by View Mode (Trinary Toggle vs Binary Breadcrumb)

Summary:

### Addendum (2026-04-17) - Legacy Tier Progress Off-by-One Fix (Include Root Node)

Summary:
- Fixed Legacy tier progress counting mismatch where displayed `x/40` excluded the home/root node while Trinary map visually includes it.

Root cause:
- Legacy progress calculation used downline-only count (`39 max`) and did not add the tier root/home node.
- Trinary render includes the root node as part of the 40-node structure (`1 + 3 + 9 + 27`).

Technical details:
- In `buildLegacyLeadershipPanelSnapshot(...)` tier progress logic:
  - preserved downline cap at `totalNodesPerTier - 1`,
  - added `baseRootNodeCount` (1 when home/root exists),
  - updated `tierProgressCount` to `baseRootNodeCount + effectiveQualifiedNodeCount` (capped to total).

### Addendum (2026-04-17) - Infinity Tier Commission Rule Migration (Requested Logic)

Summary:
- Migrated Infinity Builder tier and monthly 1% logic to the requested behavior.

Implementation details:
- Added package-based Infinity tier node reward table:
- `infinity-builder-pack` -> 50 USD per node
- `legacy-builder-pack` -> 75 USD per node
- Added `resolveInfinityBuilderSeedTierRewardUsd(...)` for per-seed reward resolution.
- Updated Infinity tier assembly in `buildInfinityBuilderBonusPanelSnapshot(...)`:
- Node completion now requires active seed and >= 3 active direct enrollments.
- Active direct count now evaluates all qualifying direct children (not only first 3 displayed slots).
- Tier completion now requires all 3 nodes completed.
- Tier reward amount is now computed from enrolled node package mix.
- Added Tier 1 strict monthly gate:
- Tier 1 monthly override is enabled only after Tier 1 full completion.
- Added Tier 2+ relaxed monthly gate:
- Tier 2+ node monthly override activates once that node meets the 3-active-direct rule, even if tier is partially complete.
- Added locked-tier guard so monthly override does not activate when tier is locked.
- Updated claim metadata `completedNodeCount` to prefer Infinity completed-node count.
- Updated Infinity UI copy/subtitles/statuses to align with the new rule language.
- Updated Infinity aggregate totals:
- `totalTierRewardUsd` and `claimableTierRewardUsd` now sum actual tier values, not fixed 150 USD multiples.

- `binary-tree-next-app.mjs`

### Addendum (2026-04-17) - Admin Data Flush Coverage Expansion (Products Preserved)

Summary:
- Expanded server-side admin flush to clear newly introduced member/session/customization tables so test resets do not leave stale user-linked records.
- Kept `store_products` (and product assets) intact per request, while still preserving `admin_users`.

Implementation details:
- Updated `resetAllMockData(...)` in `backend/services/admin.service.js`:
- added table-by-table existence probing via `to_regclass(...)` so flush does not fail when optional/new tables are not installed yet.
- expanded truncation scope to include: member auth sessions, email verification tokens, binary-tree intro state, commission containers, good-life monthly progress, notifications/read state, rank monthly progress, achievement claims, title awards/catalog, preferred attribution claims/locks, and e-wallet accounts/transfers.
- reset `charge.runtime_settings` to baseline defaults (dashboard/tier mock toggles off, legal/custom text cleared, fallback sponsor cleared) while keeping a canonical `id=1` row.
- response payload now includes:
- `cleared` counts for the expanded scope.
- `missingTables` list for non-installed tables skipped during reset.
- Updated `admin.html` Settings flush UX:
- confirmation copy now reflects broader reset scope.
- success feedback now renders a broader table-count summary.
- explicitly states admin account and store products are preserved.
- surfaces skipped/missing table names returned by backend.

Files updated:
- `backend/services/admin.service.js`
- `admin.html`
- `Claude_Notes/charge-documentation.md`
- `Claude_Notes/Current Project Status.md`

Validation:
- `node --check backend/services/admin.service.js` passed.

Known limitations:
- Flush intentionally does not clear `store_products` or uploaded product images (per request to preserve products).
- If a table has not been installed in the current environment yet, it is skipped and reported under `missingTables`.

### Addendum (2026-04-17) - Flush Button 500 Fix (Admin Auth Fallback)

Summary:
- Root-caused `POST /api/admin/reset-all-data` 500 failures to admin DB credential authentication (`charge_app_admin`) during flush client connect.
- Added runtime fallback so flush can proceed with service-role DB credentials when admin-role auth fails.

Implementation details:
- In `backend/services/admin.service.js`:
- removed hard-blocking dependency on `isAdminDbConfigured()` for reset path.
- added `connectResetClientWithFallback()`:
- tries `adminPool.connect()` first.
- on PostgreSQL auth failures (`28P01`/`28000` or password-auth message), falls back to `servicePool.connect()`.
- includes response metadata:
- `warnings[]` (e.g., admin auth failed, service fallback used)
- `connectionRole` (`admin` or `service`).
- In `admin.html`:
- flush success feedback now appends backend warnings so operators can see fallback context.

Validation:
- `node --check backend/services/admin.service.js` passed.
- direct service invocation of `resetAllMockData(...)` returned `success: true` with full cleared counts and warning indicating service fallback.

Operational note:
- If an already-running backend process predates this patch, it must be restarted before the admin button uses the new logic.

### Addendum (2026-04-17) - Admin Binary Tree Next Centered Anticipation Structure

Summary:
- Updated Admin Binary Tree Next anticipation visuals from dual `LEFT`/`RIGHT` slot indicators to a single centered anticipation node.
- Preserved existing binary placement logic and acting-root behavior (no root-model changes).

Implementation details:
- In `binary-tree-next-app.mjs`, `resolveAnticipationSlots(...)` now has an admin-only centered-slot branch:
- resolves one available placement leg internally (`left` first, then `right`, while respecting pending reservation locks).
- projects both child positions when available and renders the anticipation slot at the center line for admin UI.
- emits one admin anticipation action while still sending the concrete binary leg to enrollment flow.
- In `drawAnticipationSlots(...)`:
- added support for hidden slot-side labels (`hideSideLabel`) so admin centered anticipation no longer displays `LEFT`/`RIGHT` text.
- In `syncTreeNextEnrollLegPositionField(...)`:
- admin enrollment modal leg-position copy now reads `Auto Placement` / `Spillover Auto Placement` to match centered anticipation UX.

Files affected:
- `binary-tree-next-app.mjs`
- `Claude_Notes/binary-tree-next.md`
- `Claude_Notes/Current Project Status.md`
- `Claude_Notes/charge-documentation.md`

Validation:
- `node --check binary-tree-next-app.mjs` passed.

Known limitations:
- Centered anticipation is a visual/admin-UX change only; final persisted placement still uses left/right legs internally for binary-tree integrity.
- Acting-root model intentionally unchanged in this pass.

### Addendum (2026-04-21) - Stripe-Hosted Billing Refactor Audit + Incremental Migration

Summary:
- Audited the existing billing stack (store checkout, member enrollment checkout, invoice store, member auth/session, and settings UI) before edits.
- Preserved internal DB-backed invoice/activity history (`charge.store_invoices`) while shifting billing-management responsibilities to Stripe-hosted primitives.
- Implemented an incremental refactor: no new billing tables, no custom PDF generator, and no destructive schema rewrite.

Audit findings captured in implementation:
- Existing flow used client-triggered completion endpoints (`/complete`) with no Stripe webhook endpoint.
- User->Stripe customer linkage was not persisted in `charge.member_users`.
- Invoice records existed and were reused, but Stripe-hosted invoice URL/PDF/customer/session/payment-intent references were not fully persisted.
- Storefront/member checkout already produced metadata-rich Stripe objects, enabling safe webhook/event backfill without replacing architecture.

Implementation details:
1) Stripe customer identity + mapping persistence
- Added `backend/services/stripe-client.service.js` to centralize:
- Stripe client resolution (`STRIPE_SECRET_KEY`)
- customer lookup/create by user identity (id/username/email)
- customer portal session creation
- safe customer-to-user linking behavior
- Added `stripe_customer_id` support to `charge.member_users` via runtime `ALTER TABLE` (no standalone migration required).
- Added targeted user-store helpers for Stripe profile lookup/update in `backend/stores/user.store.js`:
- `findUserStripeProfileById/Username/Email/CustomerId`
- `updateUserStripeCustomerIdById`
- Added lookup index for mapped Stripe customers: `idx_member_users_stripe_customer_id`.

2) Store checkout Stripe-hosted invoice integration
- Updated `backend/services/store-checkout.service.js`:
- Checkout Session creation now supports Stripe customer reuse/creation and sets:
- `customer` / `customer_creation`
- `invoice_creation.enabled = true`
- invoice metadata handoff for invoice-id correlation
- Completion logic now captures and persists Stripe references:
- customer id
- checkout session id
- payment intent id
- Stripe invoice id/number
- hosted invoice URL + invoice PDF URL
- Stripe payment status
- Existing internal invoices are now patched idempotently with Stripe refs instead of being treated as immutable-only records.
- Buyer identity linkage to Stripe customer is backfilled when available.

3) Member enrollment checkout/payment integration
- Updated `backend/services/member.service.js`:
- Enrollment Checkout Session now enables Stripe-hosted invoice creation with customer reuse/creation.
- Enrollment PaymentIntent flow also links/uses Stripe customer where available.
- Enrollment completion now captures Stripe refs and syncs invoice record fields (including invoice hosted URL/PDF when available).
- Added safe customer mapping backfill to the enrolled member user account after successful payment completion.

4) Invoice model synchronization fields (existing table reuse)
- Extended invoice persistence/model support (in `backend/stores/invoice.store.js`, previously started) for:
- `stripe_customer_id`
- `stripe_checkout_session_id`
- `stripe_payment_intent_id`
- `stripe_invoice_id`
- `stripe_invoice_number`
- `stripe_invoice_hosted_url`
- `stripe_invoice_pdf_url`
- `stripe_payment_status`
- Extended `backend/services/invoice.service.js`:
- `createStoreInvoice(...)` now accepts/persists Stripe refs.
- Added `syncStoreInvoiceStripeDetails(...)` for idempotent Stripe-event backfill to existing invoice rows.

5) Stripe webhook ingestion + synchronization
- Added webhook endpoint and handler pipeline:
- route: `POST /api/stripe/webhooks`
- files:
- `backend/routes/stripe-webhook.routes.js`
- `backend/controllers/stripe-webhook.controller.js`
- `backend/services/stripe-webhook.service.js`
- Webhook processing verifies signatures via `STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET` and handles:
- `checkout.session.completed`
- `payment_intent.succeeded`
- `payment_intent.payment_failed`
- `payment_intent.canceled`
- `invoice.created`
- `invoice.finalized`
- `invoice.paid`
- `invoice.payment_failed`
- `invoice.voided`
- `invoice.marked_uncollectible`
- Event sync delegates to existing completion services (preserving existing architecture) and applies Stripe ref backfill to internal invoice/activity records.

6) Stripe billing portal access from authenticated member settings
- Added new authenticated member API endpoint:
- `POST /api/member-auth/billing/portal`
- Files:
- `backend/services/auth.service.js`
- `backend/controllers/auth.controller.js`
- `backend/routes/auth.routes.js`
- Added settings UI action in `index.html`:
- New "Open Stripe Billing Portal" button in Payment and Billing settings.
- Calls member-auth billing portal endpoint with bearer token and redirects to returned Stripe portal URL.
- Existing local settings billing snapshot form behavior remains intact.

7) App bootstrap wiring for raw webhook body
- Updated `backend/app.js` to mount Stripe webhook routes before JSON body parsing middleware so signature verification receives raw body bytes.

Files affected:
- `backend/app.js`
- `backend/controllers/auth.controller.js`
- `backend/controllers/stripe-webhook.controller.js` (new)
- `backend/routes/auth.routes.js`
- `backend/routes/stripe-webhook.routes.js` (new)
- `backend/services/auth.service.js`
- `backend/services/invoice.service.js`
- `backend/services/member.service.js`
- `backend/services/store-checkout.service.js`
- `backend/services/stripe-client.service.js` (new)
- `backend/services/stripe-webhook.service.js` (new)
- `backend/stores/invoice.store.js`
- `backend/stores/user.store.js`
- `index.html`

Validation:
- `node --check` passed for all changed backend JS modules.
- No destructive git operations used.
- Changes are incremental and preserve existing invoice/order/activity architecture.

Environment/migration notes:
- Required new env var: `STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET` for verified webhook processing.
- Runtime schema evolution used (`ALTER TABLE ... ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS`) for Stripe customer mapping and invoice Stripe reference fields.
- Existing data model/table ownership retained; no new billing table introduced.

Known limitations / follow-up:
- Webhook replay/idempotency storage is event-type/idempotent by business keys (invoice/session/payment-intent) but does not yet store a dedicated processed-event log table.
- Stripe portal configuration (what customer can manage) still depends on Stripe Dashboard-side portal settings.

## Update (2026-04-21) - Stripe Connect Recipient Payout Architecture (Incremental Refactor)

### What Was Changed

- Upgraded payout request orchestration to keep business state in-app while delegating payout infrastructure to Stripe Connect.
- Added/used app-owned payout lifecycle statuses in backend services/store flow:
  - `Requested`
  - `Processing`
  - `Paid`
  - `Failed`
  - `Cancelled`
- Added centralized payout request validation in backend service:
  - minimum payout amount (`$20`) from config fallback
  - requestable-balance enforcement (`wallet balance - open requests`)
  - Stripe Connect onboarding/payouts-enabled gating.
- Refactored E-Wallet payout request API path to call centralized payout service rather than direct table insert.
- Added Stripe transfer execution path for fulfillment and status persistence (gateway metadata + history) in payout records.
- Added route compatibility for fulfillment mode variants:
  - `/api/admin/payout-requests/fulfill`
  - `/api/admin/payout-requests/fulfill/:mode`
- Extended webhook synchronization:
  - `account.updated` -> sync Stripe Connect account flags to member profile.
  - `transfer.reversed` -> mark payout failed and restore wallet balance for previously paid requests.

### Frontend Integration (`index.html`)

- Added Stripe payout account block to E-Wallet workspace:
  - live account readiness state
  - onboarding action button
  - status/caption messaging.
- Added payout request history list in E-Wallet timeline panel.
- Added requestable-balance display (available minus open requests).
- Updated payout modal minimum messaging + min input to `$20`.
- Preserved separate commission-to-wallet transfer minimum (`$0.01`) to avoid changing existing transfer behavior.

### Files Affected

- backend/services/payout.service.js
- backend/services/wallet.service.js
- backend/services/stripe-client.service.js
- backend/services/stripe-webhook.service.js
- backend/controllers/payout.controller.js
- backend/routes/payout.routes.js
- backend/services/admin.service.js
- index.html
- admin.html
- Claude_Notes/member-dashboard-page.md
- Claude_Notes/charge-documentation.md
- Claude_Notes/Current Project Status.md

### Design Decisions

- Kept existing schema/routes and extended in place instead of full replacement.
- Preserved existing E-Wallet and commission logic; introduced payout rules at service boundary.
- Kept Stripe as infrastructure layer only (connected account + transfer + sync), while app owns request ledger and balances.

### Known Limitations

- Fulfillment remains admin-triggered in current UX; no autonomous payout scheduler was introduced in this pass.
- Admin commissions UI still presents statuses through legacy-friendly labels (`Pending/Fulfilled`) for compatibility.

### Validation

- `node --check` passed:
  - backend/services/payout.service.js
  - backend/services/wallet.service.js
  - backend/services/stripe-client.service.js
  - backend/services/stripe-webhook.service.js
  - backend/controllers/payout.controller.js
  - backend/routes/payout.routes.js
  - backend/services/admin.service.js
  - backend/controllers/stripe-webhook.controller.js
  - backend/routes/stripe-webhook.routes.js
  - backend/app.js
- inline script parse checks passed:
  - index.html (3 blocks)
  - admin.html (2 blocks)

### Addendum (2026-04-21) - Payout Service Stripe Availability Guard

- Hardened `resolveStripePayoutEligibilityForMember` in `backend/services/payout.service.js` with try/catch around Stripe Connect status resolution.
- Misconfiguration/outage conditions now return an explicit service-unavailable style payout response (`status: 503`) instead of bubbling to a generic unhandled 500.
- This keeps payout eligibility failures user-actionable and safer for production operations.

### Addendum (2026-04-21) - Auth Binding for Payout Request Submission

- Secured `POST /api/e-wallet/request-payout` behind member auth middleware.
- Backend payout-request controller now derives `userId/username/email` from `req.authenticatedMember` to prevent client-side identity spoofing.
- Existing frontend request flow remains compatible (member dashboard already sends bearer token).

### Addendum (2026-04-21) - Payout History Read Auth Hardening

- Secured `GET /api/payout-requests` with member auth middleware.
- Controller now binds payout-history identity to `req.authenticatedMember` (userId/username/email), preventing cross-account history lookup via query spoofing.
- Updated member frontend payout-history fetch to include bearer auth header.

### Addendum (2026-04-21) - Direct Payout Request Route Auth Requirement

- Added member auth requirement on `POST /api/payout-requests`.
- Controller now binds member identity fields from `req.authenticatedMember` for direct payout-request creation path as well.
- This aligns direct and E-Wallet payout-request routes under the same authenticated identity rules.

### Addendum (2026-04-21) - Instant Stripe Auto-Fulfillment on Payout Request

- Updated payout request creation to auto-fulfill immediately (Stripe mode) after request record creation, instead of waiting for admin manual fulfillment.
- Added `PAYOUT_AUTO_FULFILL_ENABLED` toggle support (default enabled) for operational control.
- Auto-fulfill path now calls payout fulfillment pipeline directly with system actor metadata and returns paid request response when successful.
- If Stripe transfer fails during instant path, service returns fulfillment error response and preserves failure state handling.

### Addendum (2026-04-21) - Admin Fulfillment Form UX for Stripe Mode

- Removed hard-required `bankDetails` input in admin commission fulfillment form to avoid blocking Stripe-mode fulfillment with manual bank fields.
- Updated bank-details placeholder copy to clarify manual details are optional for Stripe and primarily for non-Stripe/manual modes.

### Addendum (2026-04-21) - Member E-Wallet Instant-Payout Feedback UX

- Updated member E-Wallet payout success/activity messaging to reflect immediate paid outcomes.
- Activity feed now labels instant Stripe completion as processed/paid instead of always warning/requested.

### Addendum (2026-04-21) - Stripe Payout Auto-Retry Worker (No-Restart Recovery)

- Added automatic retry orchestration for failed Stripe-mode payout fulfillments in backend/services/payout.service.js.
- Retry scope defaults to insufficient-funds failures and can be expanded via env toggle.
- Retry attempts are tracked in payout statusHistory metadata (retryAttempt, trigger, delay context) so state transitions remain app-owned and auditable.
- Added in-process sweep lock to avoid overlapping retry runs.
- Added configurable exponential backoff + per-cycle cap:
  - PAYOUT_AUTO_RETRY_ENABLED (default true)
  - PAYOUT_AUTO_RETRY_INTERVAL_MS (default 30000)
  - PAYOUT_AUTO_RETRY_MAX_ATTEMPTS (default 12)
  - PAYOUT_AUTO_RETRY_MAX_PER_CYCLE (default 8)
  - PAYOUT_AUTO_RETRY_BASE_DELAY_SECONDS (default 30)
  - PAYOUT_AUTO_RETRY_MAX_DELAY_SECONDS (default 900)
  - PAYOUT_AUTO_RETRY_STRIPE_ALL_FAILURES (default false)
- Added startup worker in backend/app.js that runs retry sweeps on an interval without requiring backend restarts.
- Extended Stripe webhook handling in backend/services/stripe-webhook.service.js:
  - balance.available now forces an immediate retry sweep when positive available balance is observed.

Files touched this pass:
- backend/services/payout.service.js
- backend/app.js
- backend/services/stripe-webhook.service.js
- Claude_Notes/charge-documentation.md
- Claude_Notes/Current Project Status.md
- Claude_Notes/member-dashboard-page.md

Validation:
- node --check backend/services/payout.service.js passed.
- node --check backend/app.js passed.
- node --check backend/services/stripe-webhook.service.js passed.

### Addendum (2026-04-21) - Stripe Dashboard Button Behavior Fix (Direct Open)

- Updated E-Wallet payout-account action behavior so ready accounts route directly to Stripe management instead of only refreshing status.
- In ready state, payout-account primary action label now shows `Manage Stripe`.
- Primary action now calls Stripe dashboard open flow when account is ready.

### Addendum (2026-04-21) - Stripe Fulfillment to Connected-Account Payout (Bank-Receipt Alignment)

- Extended Stripe payout execution so Stripe-mode fulfillment now performs:
  1) platform `transfer` to connected account, then
  2) connected-account `payout` creation (instant by default, configurable).
- Purpose: align app-requested payout amount with the actual bank payout object amount in Stripe instead of relying only on balance transfer movement.
- Kept existing app-owned state machine and wallet ownership (`Requested -> Processing -> Paid` plus failure recovery).

Implementation details:
- `backend/services/stripe-client.service.js`
  - Added `createStripeConnectPayoutForConnectedAccount(...)` (uses `stripe.payouts.create` on connected account context).
- `backend/services/payout.service.js`
  - Added Stripe connected-payout config resolution:
    - `STRIPE_CONNECT_CREATE_PAYOUT` (default `true`)
    - `STRIPE_CONNECT_PAYOUT_METHOD` (`instant|standard`, default `instant`)
  - Updated Stripe fulfillment execution to create both transfer + payout and persist payout reference/status metadata.
- `backend/stores/payout.store.js`
  - Added `readPayoutRequestByTransferReference(...)` for robust webhook correlation.
- `backend/services/stripe-webhook.service.js`
  - Added payout webhook handling:
    - `payout.paid` updates payout request sync status/reference
    - `payout.failed` / `payout.canceled` marks request failed and restores wallet balance if request had already been marked paid
  - Enhanced transfer reversal lookup to resolve by transfer reference as fallback.

Files touched this pass:
- `backend/services/stripe-client.service.js`
- `backend/services/payout.service.js`
- `backend/stores/payout.store.js`
- `backend/services/stripe-webhook.service.js`
- `Claude_Notes/charge-documentation.md`
- `Claude_Notes/Current Project Status.md`
- `Claude_Notes/member-dashboard-page.md`

Validation:
- `node --check backend/services/stripe-client.service.js` passed.
- `node --check backend/services/payout.service.js` passed.
- `node --check backend/stores/payout.store.js` passed.
- `node --check backend/services/stripe-webhook.service.js` passed.

Known limitations:
- Existing payout requests already created before this change continue using their stored references/status semantics.
- Final bank settlement timing still depends on Stripe payout method and destination capabilities (instant vs standard).

## Update (2026-04-22) - Stripe Tax Manager Migration for Checkout (Hardcoded Tax Removal)

### What Was Changed

- Removed hardcoded enrollment tax-rate logic from backend and frontend enrollment preview flow.
- Enabled Stripe Tax automatic calculation for hosted Checkout Sessions in:
  - member enrollment checkout (`/api/registered-members/session` + admin variant)
  - store/public checkout (`/api/store-checkout/session`).
- Updated enrollment PaymentIntent path to use Stripe Tax calculation linking:
  - creates Tax Calculation via `stripe.tax.calculations.create(...)`
  - links calculation to PaymentIntent via `hooks.inputs.tax.calculation`
  - uses Stripe-calculated `amount_total` for PaymentIntent amount.
- Updated completion summaries to derive final tax from Stripe-captured totals instead of static metadata rates.
- Updated UI checkout copy to avoid hardcoded tax display:
  - Tax rows now indicate `Calculated at Stripe checkout`
  - Total labels now indicate estimate/before-tax where applicable.

### Files Affected

- `backend/services/member.service.js`
- `backend/services/store-checkout.service.js`
- `binary-tree-next-app.mjs`
- `binary-tree-next.html`
- `store.html`
- `index.html`
- `Claude_Notes/charge-documentation.md`
- `Claude_Notes/Current Project Status.md`
- `Claude_Notes/binary-tree-next.md`
- `Claude_Notes/public-store-page.md`

### Design Decisions

- Stripe is now source-of-truth for tax at payment time (hosted Checkout and enrollment PaymentIntent tax calculations).
- In-app cart/review totals remain pre-tax estimates to avoid mismatches before Stripe confirms address-based tax.
- Existing invoice and post-payment completion architecture was preserved; only tax sourcing/amount derivation changed.

### Known Limitations

- Pre-checkout UI totals are estimates and may differ from Stripe final totals due to jurisdictional tax rules.
- Stripe Tax output depends on Stripe Tax Dashboard setup (registrations, product tax behavior/codes).

### Validation

- `node --check backend/services/member.service.js` passed.
- `node --check backend/services/store-checkout.service.js` passed.
- `node --check binary-tree-next-app.mjs` passed.

## Addendum (2026-04-22) - Member Store Checkout BV Credit Fix (Known-Buyer Guest Flow)

### Issue

- A paid member-store purchase from username `zeroone` did not increase Personal BV.
- Live state before fix: `starter_personal_pv=1000`, `current_personal_pv_bv=1000` after a paid MetaCharge purchase.

### Root Cause

- In `createStoreCheckoutSession(...)` and `createStoreCheckoutPaymentIntent(...)`, settlement logic treated `checkoutMode='guest'` as a retail guest even when member identity payload existed (`buyerUserId`/`buyerUsername`).
- `resolveCheckoutSettlementProfile(...)` was called without `hasKnownBuyerIdentity`, so `applyBuyerBvCredit` became `false` for known member buyers.
- Result: checkout metadata persisted `buyer_bv=0`, preventing `recordMemberPurchase(...)` from applying buyer BV.

### What Was Changed

- Updated checkout build paths to resolve known buyer identity and pass it into settlement:
  - `const matchedBuyer = resolveCheckoutBuyerIdentity(users, payload)`
  - `hasKnownBuyerIdentity: Boolean(matchedBuyer)`
- Added finalize-time fallback for legacy sessions:
  - when `buyer_bv <= 0`, `owner_bv <= 0`, `invoice_bv > 0`, checkout is guest, identity persistence is enabled, and buyer identity is present, buyer BV is backfilled from `invoice_bv`.

### One-Time Recovery Executed

- Completed the already-paid Stripe session for `zeroone`:
  - session id: `cs_test_a1edtZ7Lk2t5sBVFmQErgg1VN770KjXM9WhIiAbuBZcEkaa6zTY8oSp8cy`
- Recovery outcome:
  - created invoice `INV-240930`
  - applied buyer BV credit `+38`
  - updated `zeroone` to `starter_personal_pv=1038`, `current_personal_pv_bv=1038`

### Files Touched

- `backend/services/store-checkout.service.js`
- `Claude_Notes/charge-documentation.md`
- `Claude_Notes/Current Project Status.md`
- `Claude_Notes/member-dashboard-page.md`

### Validation

- `node --check backend/services/store-checkout.service.js` passed.
- Service execution validation:
  - `completeStoreCheckoutSession(...)` returned `buyerCredit.ok=true` and `pvGain=38`.
- DB verification:
  - `charge.member_users` for `zeroone` now reflects `1038` Personal BV values.
  - `charge.store_invoices` includes `INV-240930` with `bp=38` and paid Stripe session reference.

### Known Limitation

- Webhook-only completion still depends on `STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET` deployment/config. If webhook delivery is unavailable and user does not return to success URL, checkout finalization can be delayed until manual/session completion.

## Addendum (2026-04-22) - Store Checkout Return Race Fix (Route Sync vs Session Finalization)

### Issue

- Paid hosted checkout sessions could miss finalization when returning to `/index.html?checkout=success&session_id=...`.
- Resulting symptom: no `store_invoices` row created for the session and no BV credit update.

### Root Cause

- `initMyStore()` was async and performed multiple awaits before calling `handleStoreHostedCheckoutReturn()`.
- During bootstrap, `setPage(activePage, { routeMode: 'replace' })` executed in parallel and route sync removed query params early.
- By the time return handling ran, `checkout/session_id` were gone, so completion never triggered.

### What Was Changed

- Moved hosted-checkout return handling to the very start of `initMyStore()`:
  - `await handleStoreHostedCheckoutReturn();`
- Removed the later duplicate return-handler call from the end of `initMyStore()`.
- This ensures session completion reads URL params before route replacement can strip them.

### One-Time Recovery Executed

- Finalized latest paid `zeroone` session that had not been applied:
  - session id: `cs_test_a1SrK24VGJdlzv9ziDE7TPPQFUTkFSXWT4yo1XhvAU60mdodWcpSIbozOW`
  - invoice created: `INV-240931`
  - buyer BV credited: `+38`
- User state after recovery:
  - `starter_personal_pv=1076`
  - `current_personal_pv_bv=1076`

### Files Touched

- `index.html`
- `backend/services/store-checkout.service.js`
- `Claude_Notes/charge-documentation.md`
- `Claude_Notes/Current Project Status.md`
- `Claude_Notes/member-dashboard-page.md`

### Validation

- Session completion returned `buyerCredit.ok=true` and `pvGain=38` for `INV-240931`.
- DB verification confirmed both `INV-240930` and `INV-240931` as paid rows and updated Personal BV for `zeroone`.

## Addendum (2026-04-22) - Duplicate BV Credit Guard (Webhook + Return Concurrency)

### Issue

- A single paid store checkout (`INV-240932`) for `zeroone` applied BV twice (`+76` instead of `+38`).
- Observed state before correction: `starter_personal_pv=1152` when expected `1114`.

### Root Cause

- Store checkout completion can be triggered by both:
  - Stripe webhook events (`checkout.session.completed` / `payment_intent.succeeded`)
  - frontend return completion call (`/api/store-checkout/complete`).
- Under near-simultaneous execution, both completion flows could pass pre-invoice checks before one write was visible, causing duplicate `recordMemberPurchase(...)` credit calls.

### Fix Implemented

- Added database advisory lock around checkout settlement in `finalizeSuccessfulStoreCheckout(...)`:
  - lock key: `store-checkout:${invoiceId}`
  - uses `pg_advisory_lock(hashtext(...))` and `pg_advisory_unlock(hashtext(...))`
- This serializes settlement for the same invoice id across concurrent requests/processes, enforcing one credit application path.

### Data Correction Performed

- Reverted one duplicate credit (`-38`) for `zeroone`:
  - `charge.member_users.starter_personal_pv`: `1152 -> 1114`
  - `charge.member_users.current_personal_pv_bv`: `1152 -> 1114`
  - `charge.registered_members.starter_personal_pv`: `1152 -> 1114`

### Files Touched

- `backend/services/store-checkout.service.js`
- `Claude_Notes/charge-documentation.md`
- `Claude_Notes/Current Project Status.md`
- `Claude_Notes/member-dashboard-page.md`

### Validation

- `node --check backend/services/store-checkout.service.js` passed.
- Post-fix DB verification for `zeroone` shows `starter_personal_pv=1114`, `current_personal_pv_bv=1114`.
- Backend process restarted after patch so lock-based fix is active.

## Addendum (2026-04-22) - Residual Historical Over-Credit Reconciled

### Issue

- After the concurrency lock fix was live, `zeroone` showed `1190` BV while posted invoice totals indicated `1152`.
- New paid session `INV-240933` was valid (`buyerBv=38`, `ownerBv=0`), but account totals still contained one historical extra `+38` from pre-lock duplication.

### Root Cause

- A residual data mismatch remained from earlier duplicate settlement before advisory locking was introduced.
- Current settlement logic for new invoices is correct; the mismatch was historical state, not new profile logic.

### Data Correction Performed

  - `charge.member_users.current_personal_pv_bv`: `1190 -> 1152`
  - `charge.member_users.starter_personal_pv`: `1190 -> 1152`
  - `charge.registered_members.starter_personal_pv`: `1190 -> 1152`

  - `member_users.starter_personal_pv=1152`
  - `registered_members.starter_personal_pv=1152`

## Addendum (2026-04-22) - Client-Side PV Reconcile Double-Credit Removed

### Issue

- After checkout stabilization, a fresh test purchase moved `zeroone` from `1152` to `1228` (`+76`) even though only one new invoice (`INV-240934`, `bp=38`) was posted.

### Root Cause

- `initMyStore()` still triggered `reconcileExistingStorePurchasePv()` after loading invoices.
- That reconciliation path calls `/api/member-auth/record-purchase` with `missingPv`.
- Immediately after Stripe return, `store_invoices` already contained the new invoice, but `currentSessionUser.starterPersonalPv` could still be stale for that render cycle.
- Result: client computed a false positive `missingPv=38` and applied an extra PV credit on top of backend checkout settlement.

### Fix Implemented

- Removed the auto-call to `reconcileExistingStorePurchasePv()` from `initMyStore()` in `index.html`.
- Store checkout BV is now single-source-of-truth through backend checkout settlement/finalization only.

### Data Correction Performed

- Reconciled `zeroone` back to posted invoice total after the accidental client-side top-up:
  - `charge.member_users.current_personal_pv_bv`: `1228 -> 1190`
  - `charge.member_users.starter_personal_pv`: `1228 -> 1190`
  - `charge.registered_members.starter_personal_pv`: `1228 -> 1190`

### Files/Records Touched

- `index.html`
- `charge.member_users`
- `charge.registered_members`
- `Claude_Notes/charge-documentation.md`
- `Claude_Notes/Current Project Status.md`
- `Claude_Notes/member-dashboard-page.md`

### Validation

- `charge.store_invoices` posted `bp` total for `zeroone` now equals `1190`.
- Verified account values:
  - `member_users.current_personal_pv_bv=1190`
  - `member_users.starter_personal_pv=1190`
  - `registered_members.starter_personal_pv=1190`

## Addendum (2026-04-22) - Live Personal BV Card Refresh After Checkout

### Issue

- Personal BV card on the member dashboard showed updated values only after manual page reload.

### Root Cause

- Hosted checkout finalization updated backend BV, but frontend session state was not refreshed/patched in the post-success return flow.
- KPI card rendering depends on `currentSessionUser` + `applySessionUserPatch(...)`.

### Fix Implemented

- Added `syncSessionAfterStoreCheckoutCompletion(...)` in `index.html`.
- On successful hosted checkout return:
  - patches session immediately from completion payload (`buyerCredit.user`) when available
  - fetches latest `/api/member-auth/session`
  - applies `applySessionUserPatch(currentSessionUser)` to re-render Personal BV card and related UI in-place

### Files Touched

- `index.html`
- `Claude_Notes/charge-documentation.md`
- `Claude_Notes/Current Project Status.md`
- `Claude_Notes/member-dashboard-page.md`

### Validation

- Return flow now calls session sync immediately after invoice refresh and before checkout completion feedback.
- Personal BV card no longer requires a full page reload to reflect the new purchase.

## Addendum (2026-04-22) - Preferred Account Owner BV Routing Fix

### Issue

- Preferred customer storefront purchases were not crediting expected owner BV (`38`) for store owner package.
- Affected live invoice example: `INV-240937` (computed as `ownerBv=50`, no effective owner BV credit).

### Root Cause

- Store owner resolution matched `attributionStoreCode` alongside `storeCode/publicStoreCode`.
- `member_users` are read in `created_at DESC` order, so newer preferred/downline users sharing attribution codes could be selected as owner.
- This selected a preferred account (`preferred-customer-pack`) as settlement owner, causing wrong settlement package math and `recordMemberPurchase` effective PV gain `0`.

### Fix Implemented

- Updated owner resolution in both checkout and invoice services to:
  1. prefer direct store ownership match by `storeCode` or `publicStoreCode`
  2. only fall back to `attributionStoreCode` when it resolves to a single unique user
- Files:
  - `backend/services/store-checkout.service.js`
  - `backend/services/invoice.service.js`

### Recovery Performed

- Corrected affected invoice `INV-240937`:
  - `bp: 50 -> 38`
  - `retail_commission: 4.00 -> 20.00`
  - `settlement_profile_json.ownerBv: 50 -> 38`
  - tagged recovery metadata in `settlement_profile_json` (`ownerBvRecoveryAppliedAt`, reason)
- Applied missing owner BV credit for `zeroone`:
  - `current_personal_pv_bv: 1266 -> 1304`
  - `starter_personal_pv: 1266 -> 1304`

### Validation

- Simulated preferred checkout now resolves:
  - `settlementPackageKey=legacy-builder-pack`
  - `ownerBv=38`
  - `invoiceBv=38`
- `node --check` passed for:
  - `backend/services/store-checkout.service.js`
  - `backend/services/invoice.service.js`

## Addendum (2026-04-22) - Preferred Customer Planner Invoice Linking Fix

### Issue

- Preferred Customer dashboard planner still showed stale/zero purchase data for valid preferred purchases.
- Root case example: `INV-240937` (preferred guest checkout) existed and was corrected, but did not appear under linked preferred customer row.

### Root Cause

- Frontend planner matcher (`index.html`) only linked invoices to preferred members by:
  - `buyerUserId`
  - `buyerUsername`
- Preferred public-store checkouts can be guest-mode and persist only `buyerEmail`.
- As a result, valid preferred guest invoices were excluded from planner totals.

### Fix Implemented

- Updated `collectPreferredCustomerInvoiceIdentityKeys(...)` in `index.html`:
  - keep stable keys (`buyerUserId`, `buyerUsername`)
  - add `buyerEmail` key when:
    - invoice has stable identity already, or
    - invoice is a preferred-buyer checkout (`settlementProfile.isPreferredBuyerCheckout` or `buyerPackageKey=preferred-customer-pack`)
- This preserves stricter matching for non-preferred guest invoices while correctly linking preferred guest purchases.

### Files Touched

- `index.html`
- `Claude_Notes/charge-documentation.md`
- `Claude_Notes/Current Project Status.md`
- `Claude_Notes/member-dashboard-page.md`

### Validation

- Syntax check passed for inline script blocks in `index.html`.
- Live-data match simulation now links:
  - preferred member `usertesting` -> invoice `INV-240937`.

## Addendum (2026-04-22) - Retail Checkout Attribution Hardening + Live Card Refresh

### Issue

- Preferred member purchases from dashboard My Store could miss owner BV credit when checkout payload omitted `storeCode`.
- Result: discounted Preferred checkout succeeded, but owner settlement attribution could be empty, so owner BV/retail mapping was not consistently applied.
- Secondary UX issue: post-checkout dashboard cards could remain stale until full reload in some flows.

### Root Cause

- Member dashboard checkout payload sent `storeCode: ''`.
- Backend attribution resolution is store-code based; when empty, preferred settlement could not reliably resolve owner context.
- Post-checkout frontend sync refreshed session user but did not force a member-tree reload, leaving some dependent cards stale.

### Fix Implemented

- Backend (`backend/services/store-checkout.service.js`)
  - Added implicit Preferred-buyer attribution fallback:
    - when direct store attribution is missing
    - resolve owner from buyer-linked store codes (`attributionStoreCode`, then `storeCode/publicStoreCode`)
  - Uses resolved owner for settlement package math and metadata (`attribution_key`, `member_store_code`).
  - Added `fallback_attribution_used` metadata flag.

- Frontend (`index.html`)
  - My Store checkout now sends routed attribution code in payload:
    - `storeCode: resolveCheckoutStoreRouting().attributionKey`
  - Post-checkout sync now:
    - applies buyer/owner credit patches when identity matches current session
    - hydrates member session from server
    - reloads registered members to refresh tree/account cards without reload

### Files Affected

- `backend/services/store-checkout.service.js`
- `index.html`
- `Claude_Notes/charge-documentation.md`
- `Claude_Notes/Current Project Status.md`
- `Claude_Notes/member-dashboard-page.md`
- `Claude_Notes/public-store-page.md`

### Validation

- `node --check backend/services/store-checkout.service.js` passed.
- Inline script parse checks passed for `index.html` script blocks.
- Settlement metadata now carries attribution in member-dashboard Preferred flow and UI refresh path forces post-checkout tree/session sync.

## Addendum (2026-04-24) - Mobile Sheet Surface-Drag Gesture Support (Handle Behavior Parity)

### Issue

- On mobile, the side sheet only responded to drag gestures from the grab handle.
- Vertical gestures on the main sheet surface felt unresponsive, so users could not expand/collapse the panel naturally from content area interactions.

### Fix Implemented

- Updated `maybeBeginMobileSideNavStageDrag(...)` in `binary-tree-next-app.mjs`:
  - supports mobile touch drag start from panel surface (not only handle)
  - preserves taps on actionable buttons by skipping surface-drag capture when a non-handle button is under pointer
  - excludes favorites carousel region and search-input region from surface-drag capture.
- Added drag-source tracking (`handle` vs `surface`) in mobile drag state:
  - handle tap keeps tap-to-stage behavior
  - surface tap no longer triggers accidental stage change (only actual drag/flick changes stage).
- For FULL stage, limited surface drag-start area to top region to avoid conflicting with deep-content interactions while still allowing quick collapse gestures.

### Files Affected

- `binary-tree-next-app.mjs`
- `Claude_Notes/charge-documentation.md`
- `Claude_Notes/Current Project Status.md`
- `Claude_Notes/binary-tree-next.md`

- Added external top-right mobile shortcut cluster in `drawBottomToolBar(...)` for:
  - Account Overview
  - Rank Advancement
  - Preferred Accounts
  - Legacy Leadership
- Full-stage behavior:
  - external top-right shortcut cluster is not rendered when mobile sheet stage is `FULL` (effectively behind/hidden under primary panel surface).
- Full-stage panel chrome:
  - both top radii are `0` in `FULL` stage for square top edge.

### Validation

- `node --check binary-tree-next-app.mjs` passed.

## Addendum (2026-04-24) - Mobile Shortcut Actions Now Force Center Panel FULL

### Issue

- When triggering top-right shortcut buttons on mobile (Account/Rank/Preferred/Legacy), users could land in non-full stage and lose access to vertical content scroll.

### Fix Implemented

- Added action gate in `binary-tree-next-app.mjs`:
  - `shouldForceMobilePanelFullForShortcutAction(...)`
- Updated `triggerAction(...)`:
  - when action is one of:
    - `panel:account-overview:toggle`
    - `panel:rank-advancement:toggle`
    - `panel:preferred-accounts:toggle`
    - `legacy-tier:view:toggle`

    - force side panel open
    - force mobile side panel stage to `FULL` (animated).

- `node --check binary-tree-next-app.mjs` passed.

  - nav action buttons (back/home/enter/deep) now use square corners in mobile `FULL`
  - pin toggle button now uses square corners in mobile `FULL`

## Addendum (2026-04-24) - Full-Stage Overlay Panel Radius Removal (Account/Rank/Preferred)

    - `#tree-next-account-overview-panel`
    - `#tree-next-rank-advancement-panel`
    - `#tree-next-preferred-accounts-panel`

## Addendum (2026-04-24) - Mobile FULL Stage Uses True Fullscreen Overlays (Account/Rank/Preferred)

- Updated `resolveTreeNextMobileOverlayPanelFrame(...)` in `binary-tree-next-app.mjs`:
  - added `fullScreenInFullStage` option
  - when enabled and sheet stage is `FULL`, resolver now returns viewport-sized frame:
    - `x: 0`
    - `y: 0`

    - `height: viewportHeight`

  - Account Overview panel position sync
  - Rank Advancement panel position sync
  - Preferred Accounts panel position sync.

## Addendum (2026-04-24) - Closed Sheet Position Raised Dynamically To Avoid Mobile Browser Control Clipping

### Issue

- In mobile `closed` stage, center panel could sit too low and get clipped by native browser controls.

### Fix Implemented

- Added dynamic closed-progress resolver in `binary-tree-next-app.mjs`:
  - `resolveTreeNextMobileBottomViewportOcclusionPx()`
  - `resolveTreeNextMobileSidePanelClosedProgress(options)`
- New closed-progress logic now computes closed snap by viewport height + visual viewport bottom occlusion and enforces minimum visible sheet area.
- Replaced fixed closed upper-bound usage across interaction systems:
  - snap/stage mapping
  - spring animation clamps
  - drag clamps and release projection clamps
  - frame layout clamp
  - mobile backdrop dim curve normalization.

### Outcome

- Closed state now remains visibly higher and avoids clipping behind browser-native bottom controls on mobile browsers.

### Validation

- `node --check binary-tree-next-app.mjs` passed.

## Addendum (2026-04-24) - Mobile Top Toolbar Buttons Now Support Show/Hide Dock + Context Auto-Hide

### Request Applied

- Added a mobile show/hide control for the top toolbar buttons.
- Hidden state should push top buttons to the right side of the screen.
- Trinary/Legacy view and Node Universe entry should default these top controls to hidden while still allowing users to show them.

### Fix Implemented

- Updated mobile toolbar rendering in `drawBottomToolBar(...)` (`binary-tree-next-app.mjs`):
  - Added `mobile-top-controls-toggle` button (chevron toggle).
  - Added horizontal slide behavior for top rows (`Account/Rank/Preferred/Legacy` and `Back/Home/Enter/Deep`) via computed row offset.
  - Added lightweight reveal interpolation (`mobileTopControlsReveal`) for smoother show/hide transitions.
  - Disabled row button hit targets while mostly hidden to avoid accidental taps on off-screen controls.
- Added mobile UI state fields:
  - `state.ui.mobileTopControlsHidden`
  - `state.ui.mobileTopControlsReveal`
  - `state.ui.mobileTopControlsAutoHideContextToken`
- Added context-driven default hide logic:
  - `resolveMobileTopControlsAutoHideContextToken()`
  - `syncMobileTopControlsVisibilityForContext(...)`
  - auto-hides on:
    - Legacy/Trinary canvas view active
    - Node Universe root not equal to `root`
- Added action handling:
  - `mobile:top-controls:toggle` in `triggerAction(...)`.

### Files Affected

- `binary-tree-next-app.mjs`

### Design Decisions

- Auto-hide is triggered only when entering a new matching context token, so users can manually re-show controls without immediate re-hide loops.
- Toggle button remains visible in mobile non-full stage, keeping recovery path obvious when controls are hidden.

### Known Limitation

- Leaving Legacy/Universe context does not forcibly re-show controls; the last user-visible hide/show state is preserved.

### Validation

- `node --check binary-tree-next-app.mjs` passed.

## Addendum (2026-04-24) - Half->Full Details Reflow Reduced + Top Radius Morph Animation

### Request Applied

- Reduce visual reflow/size jump in Details area when moving from mobile `HALF` to `FULL`.
- Animate center panel top border radius from rounded to square during expansion.

- Updated mobile center-panel content sizing in `drawSideNav(...)` (`binary-tree-next-app.mjs`):
  - added `isMobileExpandedStage` and `contentSizingPanelHeight`.
  - for mobile expanded stages (`HALF`/`FULL`), content sizing metrics now use full-stage panel-height reference (`resolveTreeNextMobileSidePanelFrame(...FULL...)`).
  - `panelHeightScale` and `detailsCardHeightBase` now stay aligned with full-stage sizing baseline, reducing compact->full shift.
- Added viewport clipping for mobile content region independent of full-stage scroll enable:
  - introduced `shouldClipContentViewport` and matching save/restore.
  - keeps overflow controlled while using fuller sizing baseline in `HALF`.
- Updated center-panel chrome radius behavior in `drawPanelChrome(...)`:
  - removed discrete stage-based radius toggle.
  - new continuous interpolation from rounded to square using expansion progress and `easeOutCubic`.

### Outcome

## Addendum (2026-04-24) - Closed <-> Half Content Fade Animation For Center Panel

### Request Applied

- Added fade-in/fade-out animation for center-panel inner content when transitioning between mobile `CLOSED` and `HALF`.
- Removed instant content snap/render in that transition.

### Fix Implemented

- Updated `drawSideNav(...)` (`binary-tree-next-app.mjs`) to derive transition alpha from sheet expansion progress:
  - computes `expandedContentReveal` via normalized expansion between closed and half snap expansions.
  - computes `collapsedContentReveal = 1 - expandedContentReveal`.
  - uses `easeOutCubic` for smoother visual interpolation.
- Transition rendering behavior:
  - closed-strip content is now drawn with fade (`collapsedContentReveal`) during overlap.
  - expanded center-panel content (search/details/favorites/etc.) is drawn with fade (`expandedContentReveal`) during overlap.
  - full closed mode still returns early when expanded reveal reaches ~0.
- Interaction safety during overlap:
  - collapsed profile button registration is disabled while expanded content is already revealing, avoiding duplicate hit targets.

### Files Affected

- `binary-tree-next-app.mjs`

### Validation

- `node --check binary-tree-next-app.mjs` passed.

## Addendum (2026-04-24) - Favorites Carousel Gesture Arbitration Fix (Mobile)

### Issue

- Favorites carousel was capturing touch too aggressively, blocking natural vertical panel grab/scroll gestures in that area.

### Fix Implemented

- Added axis-intent locking for favorites drag in `binary-tree-next-app.mjs`:
  - new constants:
    - `TREE_NEXT_MOBILE_FAVORITES_AXIS_LOCK_THRESHOLD_PX = 8`
    - `TREE_NEXT_MOBILE_FAVORITES_AXIS_LOCK_BIAS_PX = 3`
  - favorites drag now starts as `pending` intent and resolves direction after movement threshold.
- Horizontal intent behavior:
  - favorites keeps control and scrolls carousel normally.
- Vertical intent behavior (touch):
  - favorites immediately yields control,
  - hands off to side-nav content scroll when available,
  - otherwise hands off to side-nav stage drag,
  - using force options to bypass favorites/button exclusion during handoff.
- Added explicit bypass options for handoff in gesture starters:
  - `maybeBeginMobileSideNavStageDrag(..., { ignoreFavoritesRegion, ignoreButtonGuard })`
  - `maybeBeginMobileSideNavContentScroll(..., options)` with same bypass flags.
- Added `dragAxis` to favorites state and reset lifecycle.

### Outcome

- You can still horizontally swipe favorites.
- Vertical swipe/drag over favorites now reliably controls panel grab/scroll instead of feeling blocked.

### Files Affected

- `binary-tree-next-app.mjs`

### Validation

- `node --check binary-tree-next-app.mjs` passed.

## Addendum (2026-04-24) - Canvas Touch Feel Improved With Momentum

### Request Applied

- Mobile canvas drag felt stiff; swipe stopped immediately on release.

### Fix Implemented

- In `binary-tree-next-app.mjs`:
  - Added inertia constants:
    - `TOUCH_PAN_INERTIA_MIN_START_SPEED_PX_PER_MS`
    - `TOUCH_PAN_INERTIA_STOP_SPEED_PX_PER_MS`
    - `TOUCH_PAN_INERTIA_MAX_SPEED_PX_PER_MS`
    - `TOUCH_PAN_INERTIA_DAMPING`
  - Extended `state.drag` with pointer type + velocity sampling fields.
  - Added `state.touchPanInertia` runtime state.
  - Added helpers:
    - `clearTouchPanInertia()`
    - `maybeStartTouchPanInertia(pointerId, pointerTypeInput)`
    - `updateTouchPanInertia(deltaSecondsInput)`
  - Updated pointer lifecycle:
    - `onPointerDown(...)` clears inertia and initializes drag velocity sampling.
    - `onPointerMove(...)` computes smoothed drag velocity while panning.
    - `onPointerUp(...)` starts inertia on touch release when swipe speed threshold is met.
    - `onPointerLeave(...)` clears inertia.
  - Hooked per-frame decay in `tickFrame(...)` via `updateTouchPanInertia(deltaSeconds)`.
  - Added inertia cancellation in `onWheel(...)` and `onKeyDown(...)`.

### Outcome

- Mobile canvas now glides after a swipe and decelerates naturally instead of hard-stopping.
- Interaction feels closer to native touch map behavior.

### Files Affected

- `binary-tree-next-app.mjs`

### Validation

- `node --check binary-tree-next-app.mjs` passed.

## Addendum (2026-04-24) - Touch Momentum Felt Too Weak, Increased Glide

### Request Applied

- Reported no noticeable canvas glide after touch release.

### Fix Implemented

- Tuned inertia constants in `binary-tree-next-app.mjs`:
  - lowered `TOUCH_PAN_INERTIA_MIN_START_SPEED_PX_PER_MS`
  - lowered `TOUCH_PAN_INERTIA_STOP_SPEED_PX_PER_MS`
  - increased `TOUCH_PAN_INERTIA_MAX_SPEED_PX_PER_MS`
  - reduced `TOUCH_PAN_INERTIA_DAMPING`
- Extended drag sampling with:
  - `lastInstantVelocityX`
  - `lastInstantVelocityY`
- Updated release logic to choose stronger launch vector between smoothed velocity and most recent instant sample.
- Expanded release-start condition to include `pointercancel` in addition to `pointerup`.

### Outcome

- Canvas release now produces clearly visible glide and more native-feeling deceleration on mobile touch.

### Files Affected

- `binary-tree-next-app.mjs`

### Validation

- `node --check binary-tree-next-app.mjs` passed.

## Addendum (2026-04-24) - Inertia Not Triggering: Release/Leave Cancellation Fix

### Issue

- Glide could still appear absent because inertia was being canceled immediately after touch release in certain pointer event sequences.

### Fix Implemented

- Updated `onPointerUp(...)` to clear inertia only if the pointer event was ending an active canvas pan drag.
- Updated `onPointerLeave(...)` to clear inertia only if an active touch/drag interaction is still in progress.

### Outcome

- Touch momentum survives normal release flow and now remains visible after swipe release.

### Files Affected

- `binary-tree-next-app.mjs`

### Validation

- `node --check binary-tree-next-app.mjs` passed.

## Addendum (2026-04-24) - Mobile High-FPS Strategy Implemented In Runtime

### Request Applied

- Mobile FPS is much lower than desktop; target is smoother high-refresh behavior (90+ where hardware allows).

### Fix Implemented

- In `binary-tree-next-app.mjs`:
  - Added adaptive DPR controller for mobile runtime:
    - constants for target FPS, DPR bounds, adjustment steps, and cooldown.
    - perf state fields: `mobileRuntimeDpr`, `mobileDprLastAdjustAtMs`.
    - `resolveCanvasDprForViewport(width, height)` for mobile-specific render DPR selection.
    - `updateMobileRuntimeDprForPerf(nowMs)` to dynamically lower/raise DPR by measured FPS.
  - Updated `updateCanvasSize()` to use adaptive DPR resolver.
  - Updated `tickFrame(...)` to invoke DPR governor after FPS calculation.
  - Optimized `renderFrame()` by avoiding hidden-overlay visual/position sync calls each frame for:
    - Account Overview
    - Infinity Builder
    - Rank Advancement
    - Preferred Accounts
    - My Store
    while keeping visibility sync.

### Outcome

- Mobile frame workload is now actively controlled at runtime to favor high FPS.
- Hidden panel DOM/sync work no longer runs every frame, reducing CPU overhead.
- Effective FPS should materially improve on mobile and adapt to device capability.

### Files Affected

- `binary-tree-next-app.mjs`

### Validation

- `node --check binary-tree-next-app.mjs` passed.

## Addendum (2026-04-24) - Mobile FPS Phase 2 Hardening (Targeting 90fps-Class UX)

### Request Applied

- Mobile experience still felt capped around ~30-40 FPS; target is much smoother high-refresh behavior.

### Fix Implemented

- In `binary-tree-next-app.mjs`:
  - Added a mobile frame-performance profile resolver (`resolveMobileFramePerfProfile`) that evaluates:
    - smoothed FPS
    - runtime mobile DPR
    - active gesture/camera motion state
  - Applied dynamic frame options in `drawTreeViewport(...)`:
    - adaptive LOD thresholds
    - adaptive semantic-depth limits
    - adaptive cull margin
    - connector suppression only during aggressive mobile motion pressure
  - Enabled low-latency canvas context hinting:
    - `canvas.getContext('2d', { alpha: false, desynchronized: true })`
    - offscreen glass context also uses `desynchronized: true`
  - Reduced per-frame DOM churn for side-nav overlays:
    - `syncSideNavSearchInput()` now avoids repeated no-op style/clip updates using layout+visibility keys.
    - `syncSideNavProfileMenu()` now avoids repeated no-op style updates using layout+visibility keys.

### Outcome

- Mobile frame budget now adapts beyond DPR alone: geometry/detail/connectors can lighten during pressure.
- Overlay/input DOM sync no longer writes identical styles every frame.
- Combined with prior adaptive DPR governor, this is a stronger path toward stable high-refresh rendering on capable phones.

### Files Affected

- `binary-tree-next-app.mjs`

### Validation

- `node --check binary-tree-next-app.mjs` passed.

## Addendum (2026-04-24) - Revert Applied For Latest Phase 2 FPS Pass

- User requested rollback to the earlier working state.

### Revert Implemented

- Reverted the Phase 2 mobile FPS changes in `binary-tree-next-app.mjs`:
  - removed adaptive frame-profile tuning layer
  - restored original `drawTreeViewport(...)` connector + anticipation behavior
  - restored original side-nav search/profile sync implementations
  - removed `desynchronized` canvas context hints
- Preserved prior accepted work from earlier passes.

### Validation

- `node --check binary-tree-next-app.mjs` passed.

## Addendum (2026-04-24) - Mobile Resolution Softness Fix

### Request Applied

- Reported that nodes/icons/buttons looked low-resolution after mobile FPS tuning.

### Fix Implemented

- In `binary-tree-next-app.mjs`:
  - introduced `resolveMobileRuntimeDprBounds(...)` to enforce a quality-preserving minimum DPR on mobile.
  - minimum DPR now uses both an absolute floor and a device-relative floor ratio.
  - adjusted DPR adaptation dynamics to reduce over-aggressive drops in visual fidelity.

### Outcome

- Canvas rendering remains significantly sharper on mobile while still retaining adaptive behavior.

### Validation

- `node --check binary-tree-next-app.mjs` passed.

## Addendum (2026-04-25) - Membership Placement Reservation Plan + Pending Account Enforcement

### Request Applied

- Implement a new enrollment plan: `Membership Placement Reservation` (`$49.99`) that reserves binary-tree placement while keeping the account in a non-earning, non-enrolling `Pending` state until upgrade.

### Core Backend Changes

- Added capability helper: `backend/utils/member-capability.helpers.js`
  - constants for reservation package + standardized upgrade-required error
  - shared checks: pending state / reservation plan / pending-or-reservation
- Updated enrollment + upgrade flow in `backend/services/member.service.js`
  - added reservation package metadata (`price`, `bv`, feature flags)
  - reservation account creation now sets account status to `pending`
  - reservation member records are created with `isEarningEligible=false` and inactive activation status
  - pending/reservation members are blocked from member-enrollment routes (except admin-placement mode)
  - pending/reservation users cannot receive purchase PV via `recordMemberPurchase`
  - upgrade path treats reservation tier as free-tier origin and defaults next upgrade target to `personal-builder-pack`
  - on successful upgrade, earning + activation fields are re-enabled
  - fixed post-upgrade status derivation so legacy `pending` state does not persist after package upgrade
- Added server-side restriction checks for pending/reservation users in:
  - `backend/services/store-checkout.service.js`
  - `backend/services/invoice.service.js`
  - `backend/services/preferred-attribution.service.js`
  - `backend/services/payout.service.js`
  - `backend/services/wallet.service.js`
  - `backend/services/member-business-center.service.js`
- Restricted flows now return `Account upgrade required.` with `403` when applicable.

### Frontend Changes

- `index.html`
  - added reservation package option to member enrollment selectors
  - introduced pending/reservation state helpers and upgrade-gate messaging
  - pending users now see dashboard + tree access but are blocked from enrollment/sponsor/invite actions
  - binary-tree enroll controls and preferred customer enrollment are blocked for pending members
  - store-link copy/access and store attribution usage are disabled for pending users
  - My Store checkout actions are disabled for pending users with upgrade-required messaging
  - account status badges + upgrade CTA now reflect `Pending`
  - business-center panel/actions are blocked with upgrade prompt for pending users
- `binary-tree-next-app.mjs`
  - added reservation package metadata/constants and pending gate message
  - added pending/reservation helper checks across tree-next enrollment/anticipation actions
  - pending users are blocked from tree-next enroll modal/actions and anticipation slot interactions
  - tree-next My Store share-link and checkout actions are disabled for pending users
- `binary-tree-next.html`
  - added reservation package option to `#tree-next-enroll-package` select

### Files Affected

- `backend/utils/member-capability.helpers.js`
- `backend/utils/member-activity.helpers.js`
- `backend/services/member.service.js`
- `backend/services/store-checkout.service.js`
- `backend/services/invoice.service.js`
- `backend/services/preferred-attribution.service.js`
- `backend/services/payout.service.js`
- `backend/services/wallet.service.js`
- `backend/services/member-business-center.service.js`
- `index.html`
- `binary-tree-next-app.mjs`
- `binary-tree-next.html`

### Design Decisions

- Reservation package is placement-only and non-earning by default; commission and payout surfaces are blocked server-side.
- Reservation package is treated as free-tier origin for upgrade progression to avoid introducing a parallel upgrade ladder.
- Access model is view-first: pending users keep dashboard + tree visibility while all growth/earning mutations are gated.

### Known Limitations

- No dedicated database migration file was introduced in this pass because package/state handling is currently driven by existing record fields and runtime logic.
- Admin-side package catalogs outside the touched member/tree flows may require a follow-up if reservation plan must appear everywhere in admin tooling.

### Validation

- `node --check backend/services/member.service.js`
- `node --check backend/services/store-checkout.service.js`
- `node --check backend/services/invoice.service.js`
- `node --check backend/services/preferred-attribution.service.js`
- `node --check backend/services/payout.service.js`
- `node --check backend/services/wallet.service.js`
- `node --check backend/services/member-business-center.service.js`
- `node --check backend/utils/member-capability.helpers.js`
- `node --check backend/utils/member-activity.helpers.js`
- `node --check binary-tree-next-app.mjs`

## Addendum (2026-04-25) - Pending Reservation UI: Rank Suppression + Upgrade Toast On Restricted Navigation

  - when clicking `Enroll Member` or `Preferred Customers`, show toast: `Account Upgrade Required.`

- Updated `index.html` dashboard KPI rendering:

- Added lightweight toast utility in `index.html`:
  - `ensureAccountUpgradeToastElement(...)`
  - `showAccountUpgradeRequiredToast(...)`
  - message constant `ACCOUNT_UPGRADE_REQUIRED_TOAST_MESSAGE = 'Account Upgrade Required.'`
- Updated page gating logic in `setPage(...)`:
  - when pending users attempt `enroll-member` or `preferred-customer`, existing redirect-to-dashboard remains in place.
  - if request is user-click initiated (`showUpgradeToast: true`), toast now appears.
- Updated click handlers:
  - sidebar nav clicks now pass `showUpgradeToast: true` into `setPage(...)`.
  - quick action `Enroll Member` button now triggers the same toast when blocked.

- `Claude_Notes/Current Project Status.md`

- Kept existing backend/front-end restriction flow unchanged; added toast as UX feedback layer.
- Scoped rank suppression to the `Account Active Until` dashboard KPI to avoid unintended side effects in commission/rank internals.
